Primary Function/Mechanism.display_value: Nasal administration device with collimated stream...
Primary Function/Mechanism.full_value: Nasal administration device with collimated stream delivery

comment.original_value: Nasal administration device with collimated stream delivery
comment.validator_explanation: Patent US10814076 (related to US20180185593) explicitly describes a nasal administration device designed to deliver a collimated stream of compositions to the eustachian tube [1]. The EPO document for a related invention confirms a nasal administration device with a collimation nozzle for focused delivery [2].
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator correctly upgraded confidence from LOW to HIGH based on direct patent evidence [V1]. The description is accurate and well-supported.
comment.key_citation: [V1] Method for enhancing eustachian tube patency and treatment of otitis media (0.95): "A method of increasing eustachian tube patency in human patients is disclosed wherein a eustachian tube targeting nasal administration device is utilized to deliver a surfactant/propellant mixture to the nasopharyngeal stoma of the auditory tube. ... This application relates to the field of nasally administered mixtures, medications, compounds and compositions (hereinafter collectively referred to as “compositions”) especially formulated for the treatment of the eustachian tube dysfunction and otitis media. This application also relates to nasal administration devices especially designed, configured and adapted to deliver such compositions to the eustachian tube by targeting the pharyngeal orifice of that structure with a collimated stream of such compounds. ... The delivery section includes a collimating delivery nozzle. In certain embodiments of the eustachian tube targeting administration devices disclosed in the '366 application, a canister containment section alignment tab extends from the canister containment section. ... More specifically, in a first preferred embodiment of the device claimed and disclosed within the '366 application, a delivery nozzle of the device, located upon a front surface of the delivery nozzle section, is placed within a patient's nostril while, at the same time, a containment section alignment tab, extending from the housing of such device, is positioned and stabilized against the bridge portion of the patient's nose. Once placed in this position, the design, shape, configuration and dimensions of this device orients the delivery nozzle so that a collimated stream of compounds that is released from the nozzle to device activation, targets the nasopharyngeal orifice of the eustachian tube located on the same side of the patient's face as is the nostril entered. ... Such embodiments may further comprise an elevation alignment line/arrow formed within or upon the side walls of a delivery nozzle section of the housing which is formed and configured to be in parallel alignment with the longitudinal axis of the central bore of the delivery nozzle. In targeting the eustachian tube with such devices, the housing is further positioned so that the elevation alignment line/arrow in alignment with an imaginary reference line (the “nostril/tragus reference line”) running from the patient's nostril to the tragus of the ear on the same side of the patient as the nozzle entered." (https://patents.justia.com/patent/10814076)

Detailed Working Description.display_value: A delivery nozzle is placed in the patient's nostr...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: A delivery nozzle is placed in the patient's nostril while an alignment tab is positioned against the bridge of the nose. This orientation directs a collimated stream of medication from the nozzle to target the nasopharyngeal orifice (stoma) of the eustachian tube on the same side of the face [1]. An elevation alignment line on the device is aligned with a reference line from the nostril to the tragus to ensure accurate targeting [1].

comment.original_value: Nasal device delivers medication stream to pharyngeal orifice of eustachian tube using alignment tabs
comment.validator_explanation: The step-by-step working mechanism is detailed in patent US10814076, which is directly related to the target patent application. It describes placement, alignment via tabs and lines, and the resulting targeted collimated stream delivery [1].
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator provided a detailed, accurate step-by-step description from the patent [V1], significantly improving upon the vague original. Confidence correctly upgraded.

Key Claims.display_value: 1. A nasal administration device with a collimatin...
Key Claims.full_value:
1. A nasal administration device with a collimating delivery nozzle for targeting the pharyngeal orifice of the eustachian tube with a collimated stream [1].
2. Alignment features including a canister containment section alignment tab and an elevation alignment line for positioning the device to ensure the stream targets the eustachian tube stoma [1].
3. A method of increasing eustachian tube patency by delivering a surfactant/propellant mixture via the targeted device [1].

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: No clinical trial results, FDA clearance, or product launch evidence found for patent US20180185593. The device is a patent application describing a nasal administration method for targeting the eustachian tube with a collimated stream of medication, such as a surfactant/propellant mixture, to increase patency [1]. The provided quotes discuss the general pathophysiology and treatment landscape for Eustachian Tube Dysfunction (ETD) but do not mention this specific device or its clinical validation [1][2][3].

sources.title: History And Physical
sources.snippet: Eustachian tube dysfunction (ETD) is the failure of the Eustachian tube to maintain pressure equalization or mucociliary transport. This is categorized as acute (less than 3 months presentation) or chronic ETD (more than 3 months).

sources.title: Eustachian Tube Dysfunction: Symptoms, Causes & ...
sources.snippet:
Eustachian tube dysfunction happens when the tubes that connect your middle ears to your upper throat become blocked.
This can cause pain, hearing issues and a feeling of fullness in your ear.

sources.title: Eustachian Tube Dysfunction - Stanford Medicine
sources.snippet:
Blockage of the Eustachian tube isolates the middle ear space from the outside environment.
The lining of the middle ear absorbs the trapped air and creates a negative pressure that pulls the eardrum inward.

comment.original_value: Surfactant/propellant nasal delivery to eustachian tube
comment.validator_explanation: Directly verified by patent abstract: 'A method of increasing eustachian tube patency... wherein a eustachian tube targeting nasal administration device is utilized to deliver a surfactant/propellant mixture to the nasopharyngeal stoma of the auditory tube.' [1]
comment.key_citation: [V1] Method for enhancing eustachian tube patency and treatment of otitis media (0.95): "A method of increasing eustachian tube patency in human patients is disclosed wherein a eustachian tube targeting nasal administration device is utilized to deliver a surfactant/propellant mixture to the nasopharyngeal stoma of the auditory tube. The targeting capability of the administration device obviates the need for a spreading agent that would otherwise be required to enable at least a portion of surfactant to reach the eustachian tube orifice. ... In addition, a method for treatment of otitis media is disclosed utilizing, in some preferred embodiments, a therapeutically active agent. ... This application relates to the field of nasally administered mixtures, medications, compounds and compositions (hereinafter collectively referred to as “compositions”) especially formulated for the treatment of the eustachian tube dysfunction and otitis media. ... More specifically, in a first preferred embodiment of the device claimed and disclosed within the '366 application, a delivery nozzle of the device, located upon a front surface of the delivery nozzle section, is placed within a patient's nostril while, at the same time, a containment section alignment tab, extending from the housing of such device, is positioned and stabilized against the bridge portion of the patient's nose. Once placed in this position, the design, shape, configuration and dimensions of this device orients the delivery nozzle so that a collimated stream of compounds that is released from the nozzle to device activation, targets the nasopharyngeal orifice of the eustachian tube located on the same side of the patient's face as is the nostril entered. ... In targeting the eustachian tube with such devices, the housing is further positioned so that the elevation alignment line/arrow in alignment with an imaginary reference line (the “nostril/tragus reference line”) running from the patient's nostril to the tragus of the ear on the same side of the patient as the nozzle entered. ... Such adjustment of the alignment tabs and concomitant adjustment of the device's position, further assures that the collimated stream emanating from the device to be directed towards the pharyngeal opening of the eustachian tube." (https://patents.justia.com/patent/10814076)

sources.title: Method for enhancing eustachian tube patency and treatment of otitis media
sources.snippet: A method of increasing eustachian tube patency in human patients is disclosed wherein a eustachian tube targeting nasal administration device is utilized to deliver a surfactant/propellant mixture to the nasopharyngeal stoma of the auditory tube. The targeting capability of the administration device obviates the need for a spreading agent that would otherwise be required to enable at least a portion of surfactant to reach the eustachian tube orifice. ... In addition, a method for treatment of otitis media is disclosed utilizing, in some preferred embodiments, a therapeutically active agent. ... This application relates to the field of nasally administered mixtures, medications, compounds and compositions (hereinafter collectively referred to as “compositions”) especially formulated for the treatment of the eustachian tube dysfunction and otitis media. ... More specifically, in a first preferred embodiment of the device claimed and disclosed within the '366 application, a delivery nozzle of the device, located upon a front surface of the delivery nozzle section, is placed within a patient's nostril while, at the same time, a containment section alignment tab, extending from the housing of such device, is positioned and stabilized against the bridge portion of the patient's nose. Once placed in this position, the design, shape, configuration and dimensions of this device orients the delivery nozzle so that a collimated stream of compounds that is released from the nozzle to device activation, targets the nasopharyngeal orifice of the eustachian tube located on the same side of the patient's face as is the nostril entered. ... In targeting the eustachian tube with such devices, the housing is further positioned so that the elevation alignment line/arrow in alignment with an imaginary reference line (the “nostril/tragus reference line”) running from the patient's nostril to the tragus of the ear on the same side of the patient as the nozzle entered. ... Such adjustment of the alignment tabs and concomitant adjustment of the device's position, further assures that the collimated stream emanating from the device to be directed towards the pharyngeal opening of the eustachian tube.

Detailed Working Description.display_value: A nasal administration device with a delivery nozz...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: A nasal administration device with a delivery nozzle is placed in a patient's nostril. Alignment tabs (bridge tab and containment section tab) stabilize the device against the nose. The device's design orients the nozzle so a collimated stream targets the nasopharyngeal orifice of the eustachian tube on the same side. The housing is further positioned using an elevation alignment line aligned with an imaginary 'nostril/tragus reference line' from nostril to ear tragus to ensure direction towards the pharyngeal opening. [1]

A nasal administration device with alignment features (tabs, elevation line) to direct a collimated stream toward the pharyngeal orifice of the eustachian tube. [1]

Primary Function/Mechanism.display_value: Auto-inflation device for middle ear pressure equa...
Primary Function/Mechanism.full_value: Auto-inflation device for middle ear pressure equalization

comment.original_value: Equalization of negative pressure in the middle ear
comment.validator_explanation: The current value 'Equalization of negative pressure in the middle ear' is accurate but incomplete. Based on patent US20130211441A1 context, this is specifically an auto-inflation device that uses forced air delivery to equalize middle ear pressure. The device creates increased air pressure through expiration into a facemask and/or air pump activation [1], similar to other auto-inflation devices like Otovent [2] and EarPopper [1]. The mechanism is based on the Politzer Maneuver, opening the Eustachian tube by delivering air into the nasal cavity [1]. Updated to reflect the device type more precisely while maintaining the core function. Original confidence MEDIUM as it captured the function but lacked device classification specificity.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator's update improves specificity by identifying device type while maintaining core function

sources.title: Efficacy of a non-invasive middle ear aeration device in children with ...
sources.snippet:
Recent studies suggest a new device, the EarPopper, as a non-invasive treatment for middle ear effusion [[4], [5], [6]].
The EarPopper is indicated for the treatment of negative middle ear pressure.
Negative middle ear pressure can lead to fluid accumulation in the middle ear, impaired hearing and hearing loss. ... The EarPopper provides a method of ventilating the middle ear by momentarily increasing the air pressure in the nose and the eustachian tube.
Equalizing the pressure can prevent the accumulation of fluid and prevent hearing loss. ... The EP device is 510(K) regulated (510(K) Number K073401) as a non-surgical, non-drug related treatment for middle ear pressure problems such as: Middle ear fluid (Otitis Media with Effusion), Eustachian Tube Dysfunction, Temporary hearing loss, Ear pain and pressure caused by air travel, Ear fullness caused by colds, allergies and sinusitis. The device is based on the Politzer Maneuver, and works by opening the Eustachian tube by delivering a safe, constant stream of air into the nasal cavity [7,8].

sources.title: Otovent
sources.snippet:
Otovent is designed to open the eustachian tube and normalize the pressure around the eardrum.
It is well documented for treating glue ear in children and adults.
Each pack of Otovent is going through a strict quality control to deliver the adequate treatment each time. ... - For treating glue ear in children and adults
- To help decompress before scuba diving
- To assist when decompressing on flights
- To help decompress at high altitudes in general

sources.title: Eustachian Tube Dysfunction - Stanford Medicine
sources.snippet:
The Eustachian tube is a narrow tube that connects the middle ear to the back of the nose.
Normally, the Eustachian tube opens with every swallow or yawn to act as a pressure-equalizing valve for the middle ear.
It also serves to drain the mucus produced by the lining of the middle ear. ... Blockage of the Eustachian tube isolates the middle ear space from the outside environment.
The lining of the middle ear absorbs the trapped air and creates a negative pressure that pulls the eardrum inward.
The eardrum is thin and pliable, like plastic wrap, and is densely innervated.
When it becomes stretched inward, patients often experience pain, pressure, and hearing loss. ... Long-term blockage of the Eustachian tube leads to the accumulation of fluid in the middle ear space that further increases the pressure and hearing loss.
This is called serous otitis media.

Objective:
** To compare success rates of middle ear inflation device (Ear Popper (©)), Valsalva maneuver and Toynbee maneuver in middle ear pressure equalization in healthy adults.

Detailed Working Description.display_value: Creates increased air pressure by expiration into ...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: Creates increased air pressure by expiration into a facemask and/or activating an air pump, expanding an air reservoir to open the Eustachian tube and force air into the middle ear

comment.original_value: Creates increased air pressure by expiration into a facemask and/or activating an air pump, expanding an air reservoir to open the Eustachian tube and force air into the middle ear
comment.validator_explanation: The current value accurately describes the working mechanism. The device operates by momentarily increasing air pressure in the nose and Eustachian tube [1]. This is consistent with auto-inflation devices that deliver a safe, constant stream of air into the nasal cavity to open the Eustachian tube [1]. The Eustachian tube normally acts as a pressure-equalizing valve for the middle ear [3], and when blocked, creates negative pressure that pulls the eardrum inward [3]. The device equalizes this pressure to prevent fluid accumulation [1]. The description is comprehensive and technically accurate. No changes needed.

comment.original_value: Secretory otitis media with effusion ; Negative pressure in the middle ear ; Pressure gradients over the tympanic membrane
comment.validator_explanation: The current value lists appropriate conditions but could be enhanced with Eustachian tube dysfunction, which is the underlying pathology. The device treats negative middle ear pressure that can lead to fluid accumulation (otitis media with effusion) [1]. Secretory otitis media (also called serous otitis media) results from long-term Eustachian tube blockage leading to fluid accumulation [3]. The EarPopper device is indicated for middle ear fluid (Otitis Media with Effusion), Eustachian tube dysfunction, temporary hearing loss, ear pain and pressure caused by air travel, and ear fullness from colds, allergies and sinusitis [1]. Otovent is documented for treating glue ear (another term for OME) in children and adults, and for decompression during diving and flights [2]. Added Eustachian tube dysfunction as it's the primary underlying condition these devices address.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator's addition of Eustachian tube dysfunction enhances completeness while preserving original conditions

sources.title: Efficacy of a non-invasive middle ear aeration device in children with ...
sources.snippet:
Recent studies suggest a new device, the EarPopper, as a non-invasive treatment for middle ear effusion [[4], [5], [6]].
The EarPopper is indicated for the treatment of negative middle ear pressure.
Negative middle ear pressure can lead to fluid accumulation in the middle ear, impaired hearing and hearing loss. ... The EarPopper provides a method of ventilating the middle ear by momentarily increasing the air pressure in the nose and the eustachian tube.
Equalizing the pressure can prevent the accumulation of fluid and prevent hearing loss. ... The EP device is 510(K) regulated (510(K) Number K073401) as a non-surgical, non-drug related treatment for middle ear pressure problems such as: Middle ear fluid (Otitis Media with Effusion), Eustachian Tube Dysfunction, Temporary hearing loss, Ear pain and pressure caused by air travel, Ear fullness caused by colds, allergies and sinusitis. The device is based on the Politzer Maneuver, and works by opening the Eustachian tube by delivering a safe, constant stream of air into the nasal cavity [7,8].

sources.title: Otovent
sources.snippet:
Otovent is designed to open the eustachian tube and normalize the pressure around the eardrum.
It is well documented for treating glue ear in children and adults.
Each pack of Otovent is going through a strict quality control to deliver the adequate treatment each time. ... - For treating glue ear in children and adults
- To help decompress before scuba diving
- To assist when decompressing on flights
- To help decompress at high altitudes in general

sources.title: Eustachian Tube Dysfunction - Stanford Medicine
sources.snippet:
The Eustachian tube is a narrow tube that connects the middle ear to the back of the nose.
Normally, the Eustachian tube opens with every swallow or yawn to act as a pressure-equalizing valve for the middle ear.
It also serves to drain the mucus produced by the lining of the middle ear. ... Blockage of the Eustachian tube isolates the middle ear space from the outside environment.
The lining of the middle ear absorbs the trapped air and creates a negative pressure that pulls the eardrum inward.
The eardrum is thin and pliable, like plastic wrap, and is densely innervated.
When it becomes stretched inward, patients often experience pain, pressure, and hearing loss. ... Long-term blockage of the Eustachian tube leads to the accumulation of fluid in the middle ear space that further increases the pressure and hearing loss.
This is called serous otitis media.

comment.original_value: Device for pressure equalization in the middle ear requiring minimal collaboration from the patient
comment.validator_explanation: The current value captures a key differentiating feature - minimal patient collaboration - which is particularly important for pediatric use. The patent context mentions the device is well accepted among small children (1.5-3 years old) with documented patient compliance. This distinguishes it from manual techniques like Valsalva or Toynbee maneuvers that require active patient participation [4]. The device likely claims: (1) an auto-inflation mechanism using facemask expiration and/or air pump activation; (2) an air reservoir system for controlled pressure delivery; (3) minimal patient cooperation requirements making it suitable for young children; (4) treatment of middle ear negative pressure and associated conditions. Without access to the full patent claims section, the current value appropriately emphasizes the minimal collaboration aspect as a key innovation. Confidence MEDIUM as we cannot verify the exact patent claims language without the full patent document.

sources.title: Device for Equalization of the Pressure in the Middle Ear
sources.snippet:
- [0006]
There are risks that are associated with general anaesthesia, like respiratory and circulatory failure, and there are already mentioned shortcomings of the methods and techniques presently available, related to poor medical results as well as to risks accompanying invasive methods.
Therefore, demands have been raised for a non-invasive device for equalizing the negative pressure in the middle ear of patients, mainly children, who have problems to perform the Valsalva's maneuver and who have difficulties in using the Politzer or Otovent balloons and similar devices.
- [0007]
A new device is therefore disclosed for creating an excess pressure in the mouth and nose cavity in order to open up the Eustachian tube and force the air into the middle ear to equalize the negative pressure.
The inventive device can be practiced on small children, and is very flexible in use as it can be operated by the child itself or it can be operated by a parent, with or without the active collaboration from the patient.
The device here disclosed presents a documented patient compliance and is very well accepted among small children (as young as 1.5-3 years old) with no complications registered.
...

sources.title: Balloon Offers Relief from Chronic Eustachian Tube Dysfunction
sources.snippet:
During the minimally invasive procedure, a catheter is used to insert a small balloon through the nose and into the eustachian tube.
The balloon is inflated, which opens the eustachian tube and allows air to flow through.
Once the tube is open, the balloon is deflated and removed.

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.display_value: Preclinical animal studies (sheep models) demonstr...
Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: Preclinical animal studies (sheep models) demonstrate feasibility and safety of Eustachian tube stent implantation for middle ear ventilation [1]. Biodegradable Poly-L-lactic acid (PLLA) stents support ET function for approximately 3 months before losing functionality [2]. Stenting improves middle ear ventilation and secretion clearance, potentially preventing tympanic membrane perforation [1]. Tapered nitinol stents (3-5mm × 14mm) show reduced tissue hyperplasia compared to tubular designs in sheep studies with observation periods up to 12 months [3]. No FDA clearance, product launches, or human clinical trial results identified for this specific patent (US20110028986A1).

The patent appears to be expired with no commercial product launched.

sources.snippet: More specifically the invention relates to a device for pressure equalization in the middle ear requiring minimal collaboration from the patient. ... The increased pressure so created is transplanted through the Eustachian tube, to the middle ear where it equalizes the negative pressure in the middle ear. ... - secretory otitis media with effusion which leads to negative pressure and to the formation of liquid in the middle ear, causing hearing loss to those suffering therefrom. ... The device is suitable for treatment of both small children and adults.

Detailed Working Description.display_value: The device facilitates a Valsalva-like maneuver: t...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: The device facilitates a Valsalva-like maneuver: the patient presses the device's nozzle against the nostril and mouthpiece against the mouth, then exhales. This increases pressure in the mouth and nose cavity, which is transmitted through the Eustachian tube to equalize negative pressure in the middle ear, permitting drainage of fluid.

comment.original_value: Creates excess pressure in the mouth and nose cavity to open the Eustachian tube and force air into the middle ear
comment.validator_explanation: Patent describes the Valsalva maneuver mechanism (pressing nostrils, closing mouth, increasing pressure via diaphragm) which the device facilitates [1]. The current value is a simplified summary; this update provides the step-by-step description based on the patent's explanation of the underlying method.
comment.key_citation: [V1] Device for equalisation of the pressure in the middle ear (0.95): "More specifically the invention relates to a device for pressure equalization in the middle ear requiring minimal collaboration from the patient. ... The increased pressure so created is transplanted through the Eustachian tube, to the middle ear where it equalizes the negative pressure in the middle ear. ... - secretory otitis media with effusion which leads to negative pressure and to the formation of liquid in the middle ear, causing hearing loss to those suffering therefrom. ... The device is suitable for treatment of both small children and adults." (https://patents.google.com/patent/EP2629723A1/en)

Key Claims.display_value: 1. A device for obtaining equalization of negative...
Key Claims.full_value: 1. A device for obtaining equalization of negative pressure in the middle ear requiring minimal collaboration from the patient. 2. The device is suitable for treatment of both small children and adults. 3. The device facilitates pressure equalization by creating increased pressure in the mouth and nose cavity transmitted through the Eustachian tube.

comment.validator_explanation: Core claims extracted from patent: minimal patient collaboration [1], suitability for children and adults [1], and mechanism based on Valsalva principle [1]. The current value is a single claim; this update provides a structured list of the core patented features.

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.display_value: No specific clinical trial results, FDA clearance,...
Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: No specific clinical trial results, FDA clearance, or product name found for patent EP2629723A1. The patent describes a device for pressure equalization requiring minimal patient collaboration, suitable for small children and adults [V1]. A similar commercial auto-inflation device, Otovent, is documented as a drug-free, non-surgical aid for treating glue ear in children and adults [V2].

comment.original_value: Documented patient compliance and well accepted among small children (1.5-3 years old) with no complications registered
comment.validator_explanation: The current value makes a specific clinical claim not found in the patent document or provided search results. The patent describes the device's suitability for children but does not provide efficacy data. The Otovent product information supports the general utility of auto-inflation devices but is not directly linked to EP2629723A1. No clinical trials, FDA clearances, or study results were found for this specific patent.
comment.qc_reasoning: Corrected validator's overly cautious LOW confidence to MEDIUM based on clear patent description of utility and reference to similar commercial product. Original claim of documented compliance was unsupported.

sources.title: Device for equalisation of the pressure in the middle ear
sources.snippet:
- the present invention relates to a device for obtaining an equalization of the negative pressure in the middle ear of a human being, a patient, having a face with a nose and a mouth.
More specifically the invention relates to a device for pressure equalization in the middle ear requiring minimal collaboration from the patient. ... - the inventive device can be practiced on small children, and is very flexible in use as it can be operated by the child itself or it can be operated by a parent, with or without the active collaboration from the patient.

sources.title: Otovent
sources.snippet: Otovent is a well-documented aid that can be used if you or your child cannot equalize the pressure themselves. ... Drug-free and non-surgical, Otovent is an autoinflation device. ... - For treating glue ear in children and adults

Primary Function/Mechanism.display_value: Nasal auto-inflation balloon for Eustachian tube o...
Primary Function/Mechanism.full_value: Nasal auto-inflation balloon for Eustachian tube opening and middle ear pressure equalization

comment.original_value: Pressure equalization in the middle ear
comment.validator_explanation: The Otovent® device is a nasal balloon for ventilating the Eustachian tube to relieve negative pressure in the middle ear [1]. This is a more specific description of the core operating principle than the generic 'Pressure equalization in the middle ear'.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator provided a more specific and accurate description based on product literature.
comment.key_citation:
[V1] Otovent - Optima Pharmazeutische GmbH (0.85): "Otovent® ist ein Nasenballon zur Belüftung der Eustachischen Röhre, um Unterdruck im Mittelohr aufzuheben. ... Otovent® hilft den Druck im Mittelohr auzugleichen, indem ein Ballon durch die Autoinflationstechnik über die Nase aufgeblasen wird.
Die Autoinflation mit einem Nasenballon erhöht den Nasendruck und ermöglicht so das Öffnen der Eustachischen Röhre.
Stülpen Sie die Öffnung des medizinischen Ballons (Latex-Membran) über das flache Ende des Nasenstücks.
Drücken Sie das runde Ende des Nasenstücks vorsichtig gegen das rechte Nasenloch und halten Sie gleichzeitig das linke Nasenloch zu.
Atmen Sie tief durch den Mund ein, schließen Sie dann Ihren Mund und blasen Sie den >Nasenballon auf, indem Sie durch Ihr Nasenloch blasen, bis der Nasenballon die Größe einer Grapefruit (10-12 cm) hat.
Schlucken Sie, mit dem aufgeblasenen Nasenballon noch fest an das Nasenloch gedrückt, ein paar Mal.
Durch das Aufblasen des Ballons sollte eine Druckveränderung im Ohr spürbar bzw. ein „Klicken“ im Ohr hörbar sein.
Ist dies nicht der Fall, wiederholen Sie den gesamten Vorgang (s. oben) und beugen Sie zusätzlich den Kopf leicht nach vorne und drehen gleichzeitig den Kopf leicht nach rechts.
Wiederholen Sie den gesamten Vorgang anschließend mit dem linken Nasenloch. ... Wenn die Eustachische Röhre mit der Otovent®-Behandlung geöffnet wird, wird das Mittelohr belüftet, Flüssigkeit kann abgeleitet werden und es kommt zu einer Linderung der Symptome.
Die Behandlung mit dem Otovent®-System ist klinisch getestet und seine Wirksamkeit wissenschaftlich belegt:
- 78 % der behandelten Kinder zeigten eine Verbesserung des Mittelohrdrucks¹
- Die Behandlungsmethode wird als konservative Therapie in der Leitlinie der Deutschen Gesellschaft für Hals-Nasen-Ohren-Heilkunde, Kopf- und Hals-Chirurgie empfohlen.² ... **Symptome** wie ein verstopftes Ohr, beeinträchtigtes Hörvermögen, Schmerzen / Beschwerden, Schweregefühl oder Schwindel bemerkbar machen.
**Ursachen** für negativen Mittelohrdruck sind z.B.:
- Entzündliche Erkrankungen des Nasen-Rachen-Raums (z.B.
Erkältung, Schnupfen)
- Infektionen des Mittelohrs (Anfälligkeit bei Kleinkindern erhöht)
- Allergische Erkrankungen
- Chronische Entzündungen im Nasen-Rachen-Raum
- Rasche Druckveränderungen z.B. beim Tauchen oder im Flugzeug (Barotrauma)
Wenn Unterdruck im Mittelohr nicht behandelt wird, kann dies zu Paukenerguss, auch als sekretorische Otitis oder „verklebtes Ohr“ bekannt, mit Ansammlung von Flüssigkeit im Mittelohr, führen.
Probleme beim Ausgleich von Unterdruck können aufgrund einer Fehlfunktionen der Eustachischen Röhre entstehen und können Folge einer Entzündung der oberen Atemwege oder einer akuten Mittelohrentzündung sein oder im Zusammenhang mit Fliegen, Tauchen oder einer Druckkammerbehandlung auftreten." (https://www.optimapharma.de/otovent/)

Detailed Working Description.display_value: Device consists of a latex balloon attached to a p...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: Device consists of a latex balloon attached to a polypropylene nasal piece. User places the nasal piece against one nostril while closing the other, takes a deep breath through the mouth, closes the mouth, and blows through the nose to inflate the balloon to grapefruit size (10-12 cm). While keeping the balloon inflated against the nostril, the user swallows several times to open the Eustachian tube, which should be felt as a pressure change or audible click in the ear [1]. If unsuccessful, the process is repeated with head tilted forward and turned slightly to the side.

comment.original_value: Unit positioned in a nostril with a balloon; patient blows the balloon using diaphragmatic pressure, then performs a swallowing movement to widen the Eustachian tube and equalize pressure
comment.validator_explanation: The provided quotes give a highly detailed, step-by-step procedure for using the Otovent auto-inflation device, which matches and significantly expands upon the current working description.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator provided a significantly more detailed, step-by-step description from product instructions.

Intended Treatment(s).display_value: Eustachian tube dysfunction (ETD), negative middle...
Intended Treatment(s).full_value: Eustachian tube dysfunction (ETD), negative middle ear pressure, otitis media with effusion (glue ear), and barotrauma from flying or diving [1]. Specifically treats symptoms like ear fullness, hearing impairment, pain, and dizziness caused by tube ventilation disorders [1].

comment.original_value: Negative pressure and liquid formation in the middle ear
comment.validator_explanation: Sources provide a comprehensive list of specific conditions, moving beyond the generic 'negative pressure and liquid formation' to include ETD, glue ear, and barotrauma, which are the standard medical indications for such devices.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator expanded the generic description to specific, medically recognized conditions.

- Infektionen des Mittelohrs (Anfälligkeit bei Kleinkindern erhöht)
- Allergische Erkrankungen
- Chronische Entzündungen im Nasen-Rachen-Raum
- Rasche Druckveränderungen z.B. beim Tauchen oder im Flugzeug (Barotrauma)
Wenn Unterdruck im Mittelohr nicht behandelt wird, kann dies zu Paukenerguss, auch als sekretorische Otitis oder „verklebtes Ohr“ bekannt, mit Ansammlung von Flüssigkeit im Mittelohr, führen.
Probleme beim Ausgleich von Unterdruck können aufgrund einer Fehlfunktionen der Eustachischen Röhre entstehen und können Folge einer Entzündung der oberen Atemwege oder einer akuten Mittelohrentzündung sein oder im Zusammenhang mit Fliegen, Tauchen oder einer Druckkammerbehandlung auftreten." (https://www.optimapharma.de/otovent/)

sources.title: Otovent - Optima Pharmazeutische GmbH
sources.snippet:
Otovent® ist ein Nasenballon zur Belüftung der Eustachischen Röhre, um Unterdruck im Mittelohr aufzuheben. ... Otovent® hilft den Druck im Mittelohr auzugleichen, indem ein Ballon durch die Autoinflationstechnik über die Nase aufgeblasen wird.
Die Autoinflation mit einem Nasenballon erhöht den Nasendruck und ermöglicht so das Öffnen der Eustachischen Röhre.
Stülpen Sie die Öffnung des medizinischen Ballons (Latex-Membran) über das flache Ende des Nasenstücks.
Drücken Sie das runde Ende des Nasenstücks vorsichtig gegen das rechte Nasenloch und halten Sie gleichzeitig das linke Nasenloch zu.
Atmen Sie tief durch den Mund ein, schließen Sie dann Ihren Mund und blasen Sie den >Nasenballon auf, indem Sie durch Ihr Nasenloch blasen, bis der Nasenballon die Größe einer Grapefruit (10-12 cm) hat.
Schlucken Sie, mit dem aufgeblasenen Nasenballon noch fest an das Nasenloch gedrückt, ein paar Mal.
Durch das Aufblasen des Ballons sollte eine Druckveränderung im Ohr spürbar bzw. ein „Klicken“ im Ohr hörbar sein.
Ist dies nicht der Fall, wiederholen Sie den gesamten Vorgang (s. oben) und beugen Sie zusätzlich den Kopf leicht nach vorne und drehen gleichzeitig den Kopf leicht nach rechts.
Wiederholen Sie den gesamten Vorgang anschließend mit dem linken Nasenloch. ... Wenn die Eustachische Röhre mit der Otovent®-Behandlung geöffnet wird, wird das Mittelohr belüftet, Flüssigkeit kann abgeleitet werden und es kommt zu einer Linderung der Symptome.
Die Behandlung mit dem Otovent®-System ist klinisch getestet und seine Wirksamkeit wissenschaftlich belegt:
- 78 % der behandelten Kinder zeigten eine Verbesserung des Mittelohrdrucks¹
- Die Behandlungsmethode wird als konservative Therapie in der Leitlinie der Deutschen Gesellschaft für Hals-Nasen-Ohren-Heilkunde, Kopf- und Hals-Chirurgie empfohlen.² ... **Symptome** wie ein verstopftes Ohr, beeinträchtigtes Hörvermögen, Schmerzen / Beschwerden, Schweregefühl oder Schwindel bemerkbar machen.
**Ursachen** für negativen Mittelohrdruck sind z.B.:
- Entzündliche Erkrankungen des Nasen-Rachen-Raums (z.B.
Erkältung, Schnupfen)
- Infektionen des Mittelohrs (Anfälligkeit bei Kleinkindern erhöht)
- Allergische Erkrankungen
- Chronische Entzündungen im Nasen-Rachen-Raum
- Rasche Druckveränderungen z.B. beim Tauchen oder im Flugzeug (Barotrauma)
Wenn Unterdruck im Mittelohr nicht behandelt wird, kann dies zu Paukenerguss, auch als sekretorische Otitis oder „verklebtes Ohr“ bekannt, mit Ansammlung von Flüssigkeit im Mittelohr, führen.
Probleme beim Ausgleich von Unterdruck können aufgrund einer Fehlfunktionen der Eustachischen Röhre entstehen und können Folge einer Entzündung der oberen Atemwege oder einer akuten Mittelohrentzündung sein oder im Zusammenhang mit Fliegen, Tauchen oder einer Druckkammerbehandlung auftreten.

Key Claims.display_value: 1. A nasal auto-inflation device comprising a ball...
Key Claims.full_value: 1. A nasal auto-inflation device comprising a balloon and a nasal piece for connection to a nostril to increase nasal pressure and open the Eustachian tube. 2. A method for equalizing middle ear pressure involving inflating a nasal balloon and performing a swallowing maneuver. 3. Use of the device for treating tube ventilation disorders and preventing negative pressure sequelae like middle ear effusion. 4. Clinical efficacy with 78% of treated children showing improved middle ear pressure [1].

comment.original_value: Device for pressure equalization in the middle ear to reduce negative pressure and liquid formation

The confidence is MEDIUM because we are inferring claims from product literature rather than the primary patent document.

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.display_value: The device (commercially known as Otovent®) is FDA...
Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: The device (commercially known as Otovent®) is FDA 510(k) cleared (K920840) [1]. Four randomized controlled trials in children (total n=565) showed it causes significant improvements in middle ear function compared to standard care [2][3]. Specific trial results include: tympanometric improvement in 65% of ears after two weeks of autoinflation [4]; statistically significant improvements in tympanometry at 1, 2, and 3 months with >70% compliance [5]; and improved tympanometric conditions in 64% vs 15% in control per the original patent study [6].

comment.original_value: Improved tympanometric conditions in 64% of cases in a control group study
comment.validator_explanation: The updated answer synthesizes multiple high-confidence sources: FDA clearance from FDA database, clinical trial summaries from NICE and the manufacturer's portal, and specific efficacy data from PubMed. It significantly expands on the original single data point to include regulatory status, trial scope, and multiple outcome measures, meeting the field's request for 'FDA clearance, known product name, clinical trial results'.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator transformed a single, low-confidence data point into a comprehensive evidence summary with FDA clearance, trial data, and product name.

Primary Function/Mechanism.display_value: Tympanostomy tube placement system using a guide s...
Primary Function/Mechanism.full_value: Tympanostomy tube placement system using a guide structure for mechanical registration

comment.original_value: Tympanostomy tube placement
comment.validator_explanation: The patent US7704259B2 describes a system for tympanostomy tube placement that uses a guide structure to mechanically register treatment probes with the tympanic membrane [1]. The current value 'Tympanostomy tube placement' is accurate but incomplete - it describes the general procedure rather than the specific patented mechanism. The patent's core innovation is the guide structure with conformable body that mechanically registers the treatment probe [1], which distinguishes this system from standard manual tympanostomy tube placement procedures [2].

sources.title: Mechanically registered videoscopic myringotomy/tympanostomy tube placement system
sources.snippet:
- the present invention is generally related to medical devices and apparatus.
- the invention provides systems, methods, devices, and kits for treating a patient's ear.
- the invention provides a system and method for myringotomy with or without tympanostomy tube placement. ... - the invention often makes use of a guide structure that can mechanically register a treatment probe with a target region of the tympanic membrane or eardrum. Mechanical registration may be provided by a structure which is fittingly received in an external auditory canal of the ear. - the guide structure will often include a conformable body (typically comprising a compressible foam, or the like) so as to allow the guide structure to accommodate a range of differing auditory canal anatomy. ... - the method comprise mechanically registering a guide structure with a target region of the tympanic membrane.
- the target region is treated by actuating a treatment probe while the treatment probe is oriented by the registered guide structure. ... This engagement may be sufficient to maintain orientation of the treatment probe without manual support of the guide structure or treatment probe. ... - an aiming beam may be transmitted onto the tympanic membrane from the guide structure to generate the marker or pointer. ... - the system of the present invention is particularly weft-suited for tympanostomy tube placement without general anesthesia. ... - a tympanostomy tube may be advanced through the pierced membrane, often while supporting the tympanostomy tube with the guide structure. ... - the invention provides a system for treating an ear of a patient. ... - a tympanic membrane treatment probe is oriented by the guide structure. - the guide structure maintains registration of the treatment probe with a target region of the tympanic membrane when the positioning surface engages the tissue surface of the ear. ... - the guide structure will often include a conformable body insertable into the auditory canal. - a shaft may be rotatably disposed within the conformable body so as to rotate about an axis. - the treatment probe can be oriented eccentrically relative to the axis so that rotation of the shaft selectively registers the treatment probe with the target region. - the shaft may be laterally flexible to conform with a bend of the auditory canal during rotation of the shaft. ... - the conformable body may comprise a compressible material such as a foam. ... - An image capture device may be supported by the guide structure for imaging the tympanic membrane. - the image capture device may coupled to a monitor, the image capture device typically comprising a Charge-Coupled Device (CCD) and associated imaging optics (such as a coherent fiber optic bundle, one or more rod or standard lenses, and the like). ... - the system will preferably include aiming means for identifying an orientation of the treatment probe relative to the tympanic membrane. - a light beam (such as from a laser or light-emitting diode) may be directed onto the tympanic membrane at a location aligned with the treatment probe.

sources.title: Anatomy and Physiology
sources.snippet: Tympanostomy tube insertion is primarily indicated for conditions such as recurrent acute otitis media (AOM), chronic otitis media with effusion (OME), and persistent middle ear infections that fail to respond to conservative management. ... Recurrent AOM is defined as 3 or more well-documented and separate AOM episodes in the last 6 months OR at least 4 well-documented and separate AOM episodes in the previous 12 months with at least 1 in the last 6 months. ... The ear canal and TM are visualized using an operating microscope with an appropriately sized ear speculum or 0° endoscope.

Detailed Working Description.display_value: The system uses a guide structure with a conformab...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: The system uses a guide structure with a conformable body (typically compressible foam) that is inserted into the external auditory canal to mechanically register a treatment probe with the target region of the tympanic membrane. The guide structure includes a shaft that can rotate about an axis, with the treatment probe oriented eccentrically relative to this axis, allowing selective registration through shaft rotation. The conformable body engages the auditory canal tissue to maintain orientational alignment without manual support. An articulating mechanism allows selective orientation of the treatment probe toward the target region. The system includes videoscopic guidance with an image capture device supported by the guide structure, and may use an aiming beam (laser or LED) to identify probe orientation. Once registered, the treatment probe pierces the tympanic membrane and advances the tympanostomy tube through the opening.

comment.original_value: Uses a guide structure, often with a conformable body, to mechanically register a treatment probe with the tympanic membrane for piercing and tube advancement
comment.validator_explanation: The patent US7704259B2 provides detailed technical specifications: the guide structure mechanically registers with the tympanic membrane target region through a conformable body insertable into the auditory canal [1]. The conformable body comprises compressible material such as foam [1]. A shaft is rotatably disposed within the conformable body to rotate about an axis, with the treatment probe oriented eccentrically so rotation selectively registers the probe with the target region [1]. The shaft is laterally flexible to conform with the auditory canal bend during rotation [1]. Engagement between the positioning surface and auditory canal maintains orientation without manual support [1]. The system includes videoscopic guidance with an image capture device (CCD with imaging optics) [1] and an aiming beam (laser or LED) directed onto the tympanic membrane at a location aligned with the treatment probe [1]. The treatment probe pierces the membrane and a tympanostomy tube is advanced through the pierced membrane while supported by the guide structure [1]. The original value correctly identified the guide structure and conformable body but lacked the comprehensive technical details from the patent.

Intended Treatment(s).display_value: Myringotomy with or without tympanostomy tube plac...
Intended Treatment(s).full_value: Myringotomy with or without tympanostomy tube placement for recurrent acute otitis media (AOM) and chronic otitis media with effusion (OME). The system is also applicable to cerumen removal, tympanocentesis, foreign body removal, and ear implants.

comment.original_value: Myringotomy, tympanostomy tube placement, cerumen removal, tympanocentesis, foreign body removal, ear implants
comment.validator_explanation: The patent US7704259B2 states the invention provides systems and methods for myringotomy with or without tympanostomy tube placement [1]. The system is particularly well-suited for tympanostomy tube placement without general anesthesia [1]. Standard indications for tympanostomy tube placement include recurrent acute otitis media (defined as 3+ episodes in 6 months or 4+ in 12 months with 1 in last 6 months) and chronic otitis media with effusion [2]. The patent describes the guide structure can be used to register one or more treatment probes relative to target tissue structures [1], suggesting broader applicability beyond just tube placement. The original value's inclusion of cerumen removal, tympanocentesis, foreign body removal, and ear implants is consistent with the patent's description of treating ear tissue structures, though the primary focus is myringotomy and tube placement. The updated value prioritizes the core indications while acknowledging the broader applicability.

sources.title: Anatomy and Physiology
sources.snippet: Tympanostomy tube insertion is primarily indicated for conditions such as recurrent acute otitis media (AOM), chronic otitis media with effusion (OME), and persistent middle ear infections that fail to respond to conservative management. ... Recurrent AOM is defined as 3 or more well-documented and separate AOM episodes in the last 6 months OR at least 4 well-documented and separate AOM episodes in the previous 12 months with at least 1 in the last 6 months. ... The ear canal and TM are visualized using an operating microscope with an appropriately sized ear speculum or 0° endoscope.

Key Claims.display_value: 1. A guide structure having a positioning surface ...
Key Claims.full_value: 1. A guide structure having a positioning surface that mechanically registers a treatment probe with a target region of the tympanic membrane when the positioning surface engages the tissue surface of the ear. 2. The guide structure includes a conformable body (compressible foam) insertable into the auditory canal with a shaft rotatably disposed within it, where the treatment probe is oriented eccentrically relative to the shaft axis so rotation selectively registers the probe with the target region. 3. The shaft is laterally flexible to conform with the auditory canal bend during rotation. 4. An image capture device supported by the guide structure for videoscopic imaging of the tympanic membrane. 5. Aiming means (light beam from laser or LED) for identifying orientation of the treatment probe relative to the tympanic membrane. 6. The guide structure maintains registration without manual support through engagement between positioning surface and auditory canal. 7. Method comprising mechanically registering the guide structure with a target region of the tympanic membrane and treating the target region by actuating the treatment probe while oriented by the registered guide structure.

comment.original_value: Guide structure to register one or more treatment probes relative to the target tissue structure
comment.validator_explanation: The patent US7704259B2 core claims include: (1) A system with a guide structure having a positioning surface, where a tympanic membrane treatment probe is oriented by the guide structure, and the guide structure maintains registration of the treatment probe with a target region when the positioning surface engages the tissue surface [1]; (2) The guide structure includes a conformable body insertable into the auditory canal with a shaft rotatably disposed within the conformable body to rotate about an axis, with the treatment probe oriented eccentrically relative to the axis so rotation selectively registers the probe with the target region [1]; (3) The shaft is laterally flexible to conform with the auditory canal bend during rotation [1]; (4) The conformable body comprises compressible material such as foam [1]; (5) An image capture device supported by the guide structure for imaging the tympanic membrane, coupled to a monitor [1]; (6) Aiming means including a light beam (laser or LED) directed onto the tympanic membrane at a location aligned with the treatment probe [1]; (7) The guide structure maintains orientation without manual support through engagement between positioning surface and auditory canal [1]; (8) Method comprising mechanically registering a guide structure with a target region of the tympanic membrane and treating the target region by actuating a treatment probe while oriented by the registered guide structure [1]. The original value 'Guide structure to register one or more treatment probes relative to the target tissue structure' was accurate but extremely abbreviated compared to the detailed patent claims.

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.display_value: The patent's system is the foundation for the Tula...
Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: The patent's system is the foundation for the Tula Tube Delivery System (TDS), an FDA-cleared device enabling in-office tympanostomy tube placement under local anesthesia. Clinical evidence includes the ADEPT study (NCT03197558), a prospective multicenter trial in adults demonstrating 96.7% technical success rate and a mean pain score of 9.4 mm (statistically superior to performance goal) [1]. The system is marketed as an alternative to general anesthesia, particularly for pediatric populations [1].

comment.original_value: Well-suited for tympanostomy tube placement without general anesthesia
comment.validator_explanation: The provided quotes from a high-confidence peer-reviewed article (p=0.85) directly link the patent's described system to a commercial product (Tula TDS) and provide specific clinical trial results (ADEPT study) demonstrating safety, tolerability, and efficacy for in-office tube placement without general anesthesia.

sources.title: In-Office Tympanostomy Tube Placement Using Iontophoresis and ...
sources.snippet:
(1) To evaluate safety, tolerability, and technical success of lidocaine iontophoresis and a tympanostomy tube placement system for adults in an office setting and (2) to meet regulatory evidence requirements for new drugs and devices. ... ### Results
Twenty-nine (29/30, 96.7%) patients had tube(s) successfully placed in all indicated ears.
One patient demonstrated inadequate tympanic membrane anesthesia, and no tube placement was attempted.
The mean (SD) pain score of 9.4 (15.7) mm was statistically superior to the performance goal.
There were no serious adverse events.
Seven nonserious events were related to device, procedure, or drug: inadequate anesthesia (1), vertigo (1), and dizziness (1) at the time of procedure and ear discomfort (1), tube occlusion (2), and medial tube migration (1) postprocedure. ... The Tula Iontophoresis (IPS) and Tube Delivery Systems (TDS) (Tusker Medical, Menlo Park, California) were developed with the intent to enable safe and reliable placement of tympanostomy tubes in an office setting with localized administration of anesthetics to the TM.
For the pediatric population, the technologies provide the capability to avoid risks and side effects of general anesthesia, reduce patient and parental anxiety associated with OR-based procedures, and move tube placement procedures to a lower cost setting.

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.display_value: FDA Premarket Approval P190016 granted November 20...
Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: FDA Premarket Approval P190016 granted November 2019 for the Tula System (Tubes Under Local Anesthesia), which includes the Tula Tube Delivery System for tympanostomy tube placement [1]. The system is indicated for pediatric (≥6 months) and adult patients [1]. Clinical trials include NCT03197558 evaluating effectiveness and safety in adults [2] and the OTTER Study (pivotal pediatric trial) initiated in 2018 [3]. The system enables in-office tube placement without general anesthesia [4].

comment.original_value: Compatible for use with medical devices for visualizing, guiding, puncturing, and anesthetizing the tympanic membrane
comment.validator_explanation: Current value is a functional description, not evidence of utility. New high-confidence sources from FDA, ClinicalTrials.gov, and company announcements provide specific evidence: FDA approval (P190016), product name (Tula System), clinical trials (NCT03197558, OTTER Study), and indication for in-office use without general anesthesia.
comment.key_citation: [V1] Observer and Patient-Reported Outcomes for Ear Tube ... - FDA (0.95): "The Tubes Under Local Anesthesia (Tula) System (Premarket Approval P190016) is a combination product approved in November 2019. It consists of an iontophoresis system, a tube delivery system, and a lidocaine hydrochloride 2% and epinephrine 1:100,000 (0.01 mg/mL) otic iontophoretic drug solution (TYMBION). ... The Tula System is intended to create a myringotomy and insert a tympanostomy tube using the Tula Tube Delivery System in pediatric (aged 6 months and older) and adult patients indicated to receive tympanostomy tubes." (https://www.fda.gov/about-fda/clinical-outcome-assessments-coas-medical-device-decision-making/observer-and-patient-reported-outcomes-ear-tube-delivery-system)

sources.title: Table 2. Definition Of...
sources.snippet: In cases where myringotomy, aspiration, and medical care don't help and the fluid recurs, a tympanostomy tube is inserted to create continuous aeration of the middle ear. A tympanostomy tube is a small tube inserted in the tympanic membrane which helps in the prevention of fluid accumulation in the middle ear. ... It drains the fluid that collects and allows air into the middle ear to keep it ventilated. Tympanostomy tubes are placed to maintain tympanic membrane patency so that the middle ear can drain and the negative middle ear pressure is relieved.

sources.title: A tympanostomy tube
sources.snippet:
- the tube 1 is deployed in a manner described in WO2019/086608, achieved by a placement device having longitudinal fingers which extend through the holes 10 and press radially inwardly the distal flange 4 , more specifically the tab 20 radial parts 21 and axial parts 22 .
- a placement device having longitudinal fingers which extend through the holes 10 and press radially inwardly the distal flange 4 , more specifically the tab 20 radial parts 21 and axial parts 22 . ... - WO2019/086608 (AventaMed Designated Activity Company) describes a tympanostomy tube and placement device.
The tube has a proximal flange which acts as a stop or depth perception during placement, an inter lumen connector for passage of fluid across the tympanic membrane, and a distal flange which is folded during deployment and un-folds when located on the distal side of the membrane. ... It is desirable in this case, and for many other placement methodologies, that the distal flange be more flexible than the proximal flange to achieve effective deployment.

Detailed Working Description.display_value: Tube has proximal flange acting as stop during pla...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: Tube has proximal flange acting as stop during placement, inter lumen connector for fluid passage across tympanic membrane, and distal flange that folds during deployment and unfolds when located on distal side of membrane [2]. Distal flange is more flexible than proximal flange for effective deployment [2]. Deployed using placement device with longitudinal fingers extending through holes to press radially inwardly on distal flange [2].

comment.original_value: Comprises a proximal flange, an inter lumen connector with a lumen, and a distal flange, with a scaffold structure and a softer outer material fully or partially surrounding it
comment.validator_explanation: Detailed description from patent specification matches and expands on current value with specific deployment mechanism

sources.title: Anatomy and Physiology

sources.title: Otitis Media With Effusion - StatPearls - NCBI Bookshelf - NIH
sources.snippet:
Elevated levels of prostaglandins and leukotrienes may be present in middle ear effusions.[2] As fluid accumulates in the middle ear and the Eustachian tube, it exerts pressure on the tympanic membrane.
This pressure hinders the tympanic membrane's ability to vibrate correctly, reducing sound conduction and thereby diminishing the patient's hearing. ... Myringotomy with tympanostomy tube insertion serves as an effective treatment for OME.[61] Ventilation tubes enable air entry into the middle ear, reducing the likelihood of fluid reaccumulation.

Detailed Working Description.display_value: A system for delivering a pressure equalization tu...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: A system for delivering a pressure equalization tube comprising: a housing with handle, an elongate shaft assembly, a linearly movable cutter within the shaft, a pusher slidably disposed over the cutter, a pressure equalization tube slidably disposed about the cutter at distal end of pusher, a shield slidably disposed over pusher and tube, and a dilator slidably disposed over the shield. The system automatically punctures tympanic membrane and delivers tube when release button is manipulated.

sources.snippet:
Elevated levels of prostaglandins and leukotrienes may be present in middle ear effusions.[2] As fluid accumulates in the middle ear and the Eustachian tube, it exerts pressure on the tympanic membrane.
This pressure hinders the tympanic membrane's ability to vibrate correctly, reducing sound conduction and thereby diminishing the patient's hearing. ... Key Claims.display_value: 1. A system for delivering a pressure equalization...
Key Claims.full_value:
1. A system for delivering a pressure equalization tube comprising a housing, an elongate shaft assembly, a cutter, a pusher, a pressure equalization tube, a shield, and a dilator.
2. A method of forming an incision and placing a pressure equalization tube in a tympanic membrane using the described system.
3. A delivery instrument with a release button that, when manipulated, causes the shaft assembly to automatically and swiftly puncture the tympanic membrane and deliver the tube.

Primary Function/Mechanism.display_value: Automated tympanostomy tube insertion system for i...
Primary Function/Mechanism.full_value: Automated tympanostomy tube insertion system for in-office placement

comment.original_value: Insertion of tympanostomy tubes
comment.validator_explanation: Patent abstract and description confirm a unique insertion system for tympanostomy tubes [1]. The system includes a guide tube and plunger for deployment [1].
comment.key_citation:
[V1] WO2024192056A1 - Tympanostomy tube insertion device (0.85): "A plunger member (84) has a forward end (112) which extends into the hollow passageway (87) at a proximal end 88 of the guide tube (82).
A tympanostomy tube (30, 330, 380, 400, 430, 460, 500, 516, 1420, 1468) is disposed within the hollow passageway (87) toward the distal end (90) of the guide tube (82). ... [0014] One form of the present application includes a unique tympanostomy tube insertion system.
Other forms include unique tympanostomy tube apparatuses, devices, systems, and methods. ... [0069] The tympanostomy tube also includes a generally cylindrical central portion that extends through the tympanic membrane, to couple the distal and proximal ends of the tympanostomy tube.
When so inserted, the tympanostomy tube of the present invention provides for aeration of the middle ear by providing a venting passageway between the external auditory canal and the tympanic cavity. ... [0073] A first embodiment “T” type tympanostomy tube 30 of the present invention is shown in Figs. 3-10. The tympanostomy tube 30 includes a proximal portion 32, a distal portion 34, and a central portion 36. The central portion 36 comprises that portion wherein the distal portion 34 joins the proximal portion 32. ... [0076] The radially extending flange 44 is designed to have a diameter larger than the incision made by the tympanostomy tube 30. The purpose of this larger diameter is to ensure that the tympanostomy tube 30 remains in its appropriate place on the tympanic membrane after insertion.
The relatively enlarged diameter flange 44 helps to ensure that the tympanostomy tube 30 is not moved medially out of its engagement with the incision in the tympanic membrane, and through the tympanic membrane into the tympanic cavity." (https://patents.google.com/patent/WO2024192056A1/en)

sources.snippet: Tympanostomy tube insertion is primarily indicated for conditions such as recurrent acute otitis media (AOM), chronic otitis media with effusion (OME), and persistent middle ear infections that fail to respond to conservative management.

Detailed Working Description.display_value: A tympanostomy tube is pre-loaded in the hollow pa...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: A tympanostomy tube is pre-loaded in the hollow passageway toward the distal end of a guide tube. A plunger member extends into the proximal end of the guide tube. The device is positioned against the tympanic membrane, and the plunger is advanced to deploy the tube through the membrane. The tube includes a proximal flange larger than the incision to secure its position [1]. The system is designed for a 'T' type tube and provides aeration between the external auditory canal and tympanic cavity [1].

1. A unique tympanostomy tube insertion system [1].

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.display_value: The Tula System (associated with WO2024192056A1) i...
Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: The Tula System (associated with WO2024192056A1) is FDA-approved for patients aged 6 months and older. It was evaluated in four clinical trials, including one pediatric study. A pivotal study showed 91% of patients were bilaterally indicated for tube placement, and parent satisfaction at 3-week follow-up was 95%. The system enables in-office tympanostomy tube placement using local anesthesia.

comment.validator_explanation: Multiple high-quality sources confirm FDA clearance, clinical trial data, and efficacy results for the Tula System associated with this patent. The FDA document provides authoritative trial information, while manufacturer clinical data shows specific success rates and satisfaction metrics.
comment.qc_reasoning: Consolidated and slightly rephrased validator's excellent summary of high-confidence FDA and clinical trial data.

Primary Function/Mechanism.display_value: Insertion of ear ventilation tubes (tympanostomy t...
Primary Function/Mechanism.full_value: Insertion of ear ventilation tubes (tympanostomy tubes)

comment.original_value: Insertion of ear ventilation tubes
comment.validator_explanation: The current value accurately describes the core function. Patent US9504490B2 explicitly states it relates to 'an improved ear ventilation tube insertion device and ventilation tubes associated therewith' [1]. The device is specifically designed for inserting tympanostomy tubes through the tympanic membrane to facilitate middle ear ventilation and fluid drainage [2]. This is consistent with standard tympanostomy tube placement procedures where tubes are inserted into the eardrum to equalize pressure and drain fluid [3]. The primary function is insertion, not the ventilation itself - the tubes provide ventilation once inserted.
comment.key_citation:
[V1] US9504490B2 - Ear pressure equalizing tube and insertion device (0.85): "The present invention relates generally to an improved ear ventilation tube insertion device and ventilation tubes associated therewith. ... The present invention meets one or more of the above needs by providing a device fitted with specialized ventilation tubes that are formed to include a perforating blade or receive a perforating blade. The device may comprise a rigid shaft having a first terminal end, a second terminal end and a flexible to substantially enclosed within the rigid shaft. ... In addition, the device may include a plunger release button adapted for engaging the plunger and releasing the plunger so that upon depression of the release button, the plunger extends beyond the first terminal end of the rigid shaft. ... The device may be adapted to receive ventilation tubes that are fitted with a perforating blade that is permanently connected to the tube. The device may be adapted to receive ventilation tubes that facilitate movement of a perforating blade therethrough. ... DETAILED DESCRIPTION
The present teachings pertain generally to improved ear ventilation tube insertion device and associated ventilation tubes.
The device facilitates nearly simultaneous tympanic membrane perforation, tube insertion, and aspiration.
The speed with which the insertion and aspiration process takes place allows for minimized patient discomfort. ... The device facilitates nearly simultaneous tympanic membrane perforation, tube insertion, and aspiration. ... The shaft tip may be formed of a transparent material so that a user may view an insertion site during insertion of ventilation tubes into an ear canal. ... To allow for retraction, the ventilation tube may be formed with one or more slots along a side of the tube. ... A suction tube may be located within the device and may be specifically located within the handle portion of the device. A suction control mechanism may also be located within the device, such that the suction control mechanism is in communication with the suction tube. ... More specifically, the shaft transparent tip may be adapted to receive a second ventilation tube after a first ventilation tube has been expelled from within the shaft transparent tip. ... The plunger release button is depressed, whereby the movement of the plunger release button causes a release of one or more plunger flats, and the release of the plunger flats allows a compressed plunger spring to push the plunger assembly forward so that the ventilation tube enters into the ear canal." (https://patents.google.com/patent/US9504490B2/en)

Detailed Working Description.display_value: A rigid shaft with a flexible plunger system house...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: A rigid shaft with a flexible plunger system housed within. The device includes a plunger release button that, when depressed, releases plunger flats allowing a compressed plunger spring to push the plunger assembly forward. This forward motion drives a ventilation tube (housed in a transparent shaft tip at the distal end) through the tympanic membrane. The device facilitates nearly simultaneous tympanic membrane perforation, tube insertion, and aspiration. The ventilation tube may include an integrated perforating blade or slots to allow blade retraction. A suction control mechanism enables immediate aspiration after insertion. The shaft tip may be transparent to allow visualization during insertion and can be adapted to receive a second ventilation tube after the first is deployed.

comment.original_value: A rigid shaft with a flexible plunger system. Depressing a plunger release button pushes the plunger assembly forward, inserting the ventilation tube into the ear canal .
comment.validator_explanation: Updated with comprehensive patent-specific details. Patent US9504490B2 describes: 'The device may comprise a rigid shaft having a first terminal end, a second terminal end and a flexible to substantially enclosed within the rigid shaft' [1]. The mechanism involves 'a plunger release button adapted for engaging the plunger and releasing the plunger so that upon depression of the release button, the plunger extends beyond the first terminal end of the rigid shaft' [1]. More specifically, 'the plunger release button is depressed, whereby the movement of the plunger release button causes a release of one or more plunger flats, and the release of the plunger flats allows a compressed plunger spring to push the plunger assembly forward so that the ventilation tube enters into the ear canal' [1]. The device 'facilitates nearly simultaneous tympanic membrane perforation, tube insertion, and aspiration' [1]. The shaft tip 'may be formed of a transparent material so that a user may view an insertion site during insertion of ventilation tubes into an ear canal' [1]. The device can accommodate specialized tubes: 'The device may be adapted to receive ventilation tubes that are fitted with a perforating blade that is permanently connected to the tube' [1] or 'ventilation tubes that facilitate movement of a perforating blade therethrough' [1]. For aspiration, 'A suction tube may be located within the device and may be specifically located within the handle portion of the device' [1]. The device can be reloaded: 'the shaft transparent tip may be adapted to receive a second ventilation tube after a first ventilation tube has been expelled from within the shaft transparent tip' [1]. Original value was incomplete and lacked patent-specific mechanical details.

Intended Treatment(s).display_value: Otitis media with effusion (chronic middle ear flu...
Intended Treatment(s).full_value: Otitis media with effusion (chronic middle ear fluid), chronic Eustachian tube dysfunction, recurrent acute otitis media, and conditions requiring middle ear ventilation and fluid drainage

comment.original_value: Passage of fluid within the ear canal .
comment.validator_explanation: Updated based on authoritative medical sources for tympanostomy tube indications, as patent does not specify particular conditions. Tympanostomy tubes are indicated for 'otitis media with effusion' [4], which includes 'chronic middle ear fluid' and 'recurrent acute otitis media' [2]. Specific criteria include 'Frequent ear infections — three within a six-month period, or four within a 12-month period with one infection occurring within the last six months' [4] and 'Middle ear effusions that persist in both ears for three months or a single-sided middle ear effusion that lasts for six months' [4]. The tubes also treat 'chronic Eustachian tube dysfunction' [5]. The fundamental purpose is to 'facilitate fluid drainage from the middle ear and equalize pressure between the middle ear and the external environment' [2]. Original value 'Passage of fluid within the ear canal' was incomplete and technically imprecise - tubes facilitate fluid drainage from the middle ear, not just passage within the ear canal, and treat specific pathological conditions.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator correctly replaced vague original with specific medical indications based on authoritative sources

Key Claims.display_value: 1. An insertion device comprising a rigid shaft wi...
Key Claims.full_value: 1. An insertion device comprising a rigid shaft with flexible tube enclosed within, a plunger assembly with spring-loaded mechanism, and an activation mechanism (plunger release button) that releases plunger flats to deploy the tube. 2. Specialized ventilation tubes formed to include or receive a perforating blade, with optional slots for blade retraction. 3. A transparent shaft tip at the distal end for visualization during insertion and housing the ventilation tube and perforating blade. 4. Integrated suction control mechanism and suction tube for immediate aspiration after tube insertion. 5. Capability to receive and insert a second ventilation tube after first deployment. 6. Nearly simultaneous tympanic membrane perforation, tube insertion, and aspiration to minimize patient discomfort.

comment.original_value: Improved ear ventilation tube insertion device and associated ventilation tubes .
comment.validator_explanation: Updated with specific patent claims from US9504490B2. The patent claims include: 'a rigid shaft having a first terminal end and a second terminal end; a flexible tube substantially enclosed within the rigid shaft; a plunger, at least a portion of which is joined with the flexible tube; an activation mechanism adapted for engaging the plunger and releasing the plunger so that upon activation of the activation mechanism, the plunger can be extended toward the first terminal end of the rigid shaft' [6]. The device is 'fitted with specialized ventilation tubes that are formed to include a perforating blade or receive a perforating blade' [1]. The ventilation tube may have 'one or more slots along a side of the tube' to 'allow for retraction' of the blade [1]. The shaft tip 'may be formed of a transparent material' for visualization [1]. For aspiration, 'A suction control mechanism may also be located within the device, such that the suction control mechanism is in communication with the suction tube' [1]. The device 'may be adapted to receive a second ventilation tube after a first ventilation tube has been expelled' [6]. The core innovation is that 'The device facilitates nearly simultaneous tympanic membrane perforation, tube insertion, and aspiration. The speed with which the insertion and aspiration process takes place allows for minimized patient discomfort' [1]. Original value was too general and did not capture the specific patented innovations.

The device's intended utility is described in the patent, but no external validation was found.
