comment.key_citation: [V1] Tympanic membrane pressure equalization tube delivery system (0.95): "- the present invention provides systems and methods for automatically puncturing and delivering a tympanic membrane equalization tube (i.e., tympanostomy tube) into a tympanic membrane. ... - such systems and methods facilitate automatic delivery of a tympanic membrane equalization tube with minimal steps to be performed by an operator, such as advancing the system into an ear canal and triggering a release button. ... The mechanism can be triggered to initiate a fast and automatic process which punctures the tympanic membrane, and delivers the tympanic membrane equalization tube. ... - Embodiments of the invention are compatible for use with a suite of medical devices for visualizing, guiding other medical devices, delivering a tympanic membrane equalization tube, puncturing the tympanic membrane, and anesthetizing the tympanic membrane. ... The release button 106 is configured to release an internal mechanism which causes the shaft assembly 104 to automatically puncture a tympanic membrane and also insert the tympanic membrane equalization tube into the punctured tympanic membrane." (https://patents.google.com/patent/US20110015645A1/en)

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: The Tula Tube Delivery System (TDS) was evaluated in the ADEPT clinical trial (NCT03197558), a prospective multicenter single-arm study. Results showed 96.7% (29/30) technical success rate for tube placement in adults, with a mean pain score of 9.4 mm (statistically superior to performance goal of 45 mm) and no serious adverse events [1]. The system was developed to enable safe, reliable in-office tube placement, avoiding general anesthesia risks for pediatric populations [1].

comment.original_value: Facilitates automatic delivery with minimal steps .
comment.validator_explanation: Clinical trial data from a peer-reviewed study (PMC7040928) provides specific efficacy and safety results, significantly improving upon the original vague description.
comment.key_citation:
[V1] In-Office Tympanostomy Tube Placement Using Iontophoresis and ... (0.85): "### Results
Twenty-nine (29/30, 96.7%) patients had tube(s) successfully placed in all indicated ears.
One patient demonstrated inadequate tympanic membrane anesthesia, and no tube placement was attempted.
The mean (SD) pain score of 9.4 (15.7) mm was statistically superior to the performance goal.
There were no serious adverse events.
Seven nonserious events were related to device, procedure, or drug: inadequate anesthesia (1), vertigo (1), and dizziness (1) at the time of procedure and ear discomfort (1), tube occlusion (2), and medial tube migration (1) postprocedure. ... The Tula Iontophoresis (IPS) and Tube Delivery Systems (TDS) (Tusker Medical, Menlo Park, California) were developed with the intent to enable safe and reliable placement of tympanostomy tubes in an office setting with localized administration of anesthetics to the TM.
For the pediatric population, the technologies provide the capability to avoid risks and side effects of general anesthesia, reduce patient and parental anxiety associated with OR-based procedures, and move tube placement procedures to a lower cost setting." (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7040928/)

sources.title: In-Office Tympanostomy Tube Placement Using Iontophoresis and ...
sources.snippet:
### Results
Twenty-nine (29/30, 96.7%) patients had tube(s) successfully placed in all indicated ears.
One patient demonstrated inadequate tympanic membrane anesthesia, and no tube placement was attempted.
The mean (SD) pain score of 9.4 (15.7) mm was statistically superior to the performance goal.
There were no serious adverse events.
Seven nonserious events were related to device, procedure, or drug: inadequate anesthesia (1), vertigo (1), and dizziness (1) at the time of procedure and ear discomfort (1), tube occlusion (2), and medial tube migration (1) postprocedure. ... The Tula Iontophoresis (IPS) and Tube Delivery Systems (TDS) (Tusker Medical, Menlo Park, California) were developed with the intent to enable safe and reliable placement of tympanostomy tubes in an office setting with localized administration of anesthetics to the TM.
For the pediatric population, the technologies provide the capability to avoid risks and side effects of general anesthesia, reduce patient and parental anxiety associated with OR-based procedures, and move tube placement procedures to a lower cost setting.

Stenting the ET could improve ventilation of the middle ear and clearance of secretion through the ET itself, preventing tympanic membrane perforation and over the long term, middle ear destruction. ... (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5935938/)

sources.title: Frontiers | First in vivo test of a biodegradable polymeric stent for the Eustachian tube in sheep
sources.snippet:
Biodegradable stents have been developed that support the impaired ET function until natural ventilation of the middle ear via the ET is restored, and dissolve after their function has been fulfilled. ... **Methods:** Biodegradable stents (diameter: 3.5 mm; length: 15 mm) were made from Poly-L-lactic acid (PLLA) in a dip-coating process and subsequent laser cutting and were crimped on a balloon catheter.
Stent implantation into the ET was performed unilaterally in nine healthy female blackface sheep under endoscopic control with a dedicated insertion tool and an inflation pressure of 10 bar for 2 min.

sources.title: First Investigation of a Eustachian Tube Stent in ...
sources.snippet:
Unmet needs in the treatment of chronic otitis media and Eustachian tube dysfunction (ETD) triggered the development of stents for the Eustachian tube (ET).
In this study, for the first time, stents were placed in an artificially blocked ET to evaluate stent function.

sources.title: Eustachian Tube Balloon Dilation | Mount Sinai - New York
sources.snippet:
## About Chronic Eustachian Tube Dysfunction
The Eustachian tube protects the middle ear by opening and closing throughout the day -- equalizing the pressure within the ear and the outside world.
You may notice this when flying in a plane, climbing a mountain or diving underwater and the pressure builds within the ear until you hear a “pop”.
The release or pop is made possible by the Eustachian tube opening and equalizing the pressure.
Individuals with ETD have a chronic inability to equalize air pressure, resulting in pain, a feeling of fullness, ringing or popping noises, or even hearing loss. ... ## How Eustachian Tube Balloon Dilation is Performed
Before the procedure, your surgeon will place a small endoscope — a thin tube with a camera and light — through the nasal opening to examine the nasal cavity and look for any potential causes of ETD or assess for anatomical issues that may impact the treatment setting for you.
Examples of these include ruling out sinus infections, extent of acid reflux, severity of allergic rhinitis, presence of septal deviations, etc.
On the day of your procedure, an endoscope is introduced into the nose with the deflated Eustachian tube balloon.
The device is guided through the nasal passageway until it reaches the eustachian tube opening in the back of the nose.
Under direct visualization, the balloon is then inserted into the Eustachian tube and inflated to dilate the blocked tube — similar to the way a stent is used to open up a clogged artery.
The balloon is kept inflated for about two minutes helping to remodel the Eustachian tube cartilage and flatten out any inflammation.
The balloon is then deflated and removed from the Eustachian tube.

sources.title: Balloon dilation of the Eustachian tube: clinical experience ...
sources.snippet:
## SUMMARY
Balloon dilation of the Eustachian tube has been recently introduced as a novel and minimally invasive method for treating chronic obstructive Eustachian tube dysfunction.
For the first time worldwide, we assessed the role of this technique in the treatment of children with Eustachian tube dysfunction who did not respond to other treatments. ... We used a modification of the technique that was originally described by Sudhoff et al. ^3^ ^6^.
Laryngeal mask airways were generally used during anaesthesia.
A xylometazoline solution was used for nasal decongestion.
A 30° Hopkins rod was then inserted on the side to be treated.
An insertion instrument was carefully introduced on the contralateral side.
A balloon catheter was passed through the insertion instrument and pushed 2 cm into the Eustachian tube under endoscopic vision.
The balloon was inflated to a pressure of 10 bar for 2 min.

sources.title: Acclarent Announces U.S. Launch of ACCLARENT AERA ...
sources.snippet:
“Health care providers treating ETD frequently see ear infections, pressure or blockage, or complaints such as hearing loss and pain,” said Dr. Dennis S.
Poe, professor of otolaryngology at Harvard Medical School and Boston Children’s Hospital. “As discussed at the recent American Academy of Otolaryngology annual meeting, doctors do their best to treat these conditions, but until now – with the launch of ACCLARENT AERA™ – options did not address the root of the problem with the Eustachian tube itself.”

Detailed Working Description.display_value: Device constructed from biocompatible and bioresor...

sources.snippet: Therefore, this study investigated a tapered nitinol stent (3–5 mm × 14 mm) for the human ET as a potential new permanent treatment for chronic Eustachian tube dysfunction (ETD) and thus ME ventilation disorders.

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.display_value: No specific clinical trial results, FDA clearance,...
Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: No specific clinical trial results, FDA clearance, or product launch evidence found for patent US20110028986A1. General evidence for balloon dilation of the Eustachian tube (BDET) includes: FDA 510(k) clearance for commercial systems like XprESS® ENT Dilation System (2017) and Next Generation Balloon Dilation System (2020) [1]. The ACCLARENT AERA™ system was the first FDA-approved balloon dilation device for ETD, launched in 2016 [2]. Randomized controlled trials show BDET plus medical management significantly improves patient-reported symptoms (ETDQ-7) and tympanogram normalization vs. medical management alone at 6 weeks, with durability at 52 weeks [3]. A pivotal trial for the AERA system showed 52% of patients had normalized tympanograms at 6 weeks vs. 14% with medical management alone [4]. The procedure is described as providing relief from ear pressure, fluid, and congestion [1][5].

comment.original_value: Provides immediate relief from fluid and pressure .
comment.validator_explanation: Searches for the specific patent number did not yield direct clinical evidence. However, high-quality general evidence for the therapeutic approach (balloon dilation) was found, including FDA clearances for commercial systems, clinical trial results showing efficacy, and product launch information. The original vague claim is not directly verifiable for this specific patent application.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator correctly identified that no direct evidence was found for the specific patent (US20110028986A1). The provided answer is a well-synthesized summary of general clinical evidence for the therapeutic approach (balloon dilation), including FDA clearances and trial results. This is a major, informative update from the original vague claim.

sources.title: CG-SURG-117 Balloon Dilation of the Eustachian Tubes
sources.snippet:
The FDA granted 510k clearance for the XprESS ® ENT Dilation System in 2017 for adults 18 years and older.
The Next Generation Balloon Dilation System was cleared by the FDA in 2020 for use with adults 18 years and older. ... It is proposed to relieve chronic ear congestion and middle ear and mastoid infections.

sources.title: Acclarent Announces U.S. Launch of ACCLARENT AERA ...
sources.snippet:
**Irvine, Calif., September 19, 2016** – Acclarent, Inc., today announced the U.S. availability of the ACCLARENT AERA™ Eustachian Tube Balloon Dilation System, the first balloon dilation intervention approved by the U.S.
Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for Eustachian Tube Dysfunction (ETD).

sources.title: Balloon Dilation of the Eustachian Tube
sources.snippet: Two 6-week RCTs found more improvement with balloon dilation plus medical management than medical management alone on patient-reported symptoms, ability to perform a Valsalva maneuver, proportion of patients with normalized tympanograms, and otoscopy findings. ... Durability of these effects was demonstrated at 52 weeks in the uncontrolled extension phase of both RCTs.

sources.title: FDA permits marketing of balloon device to treat persistent Eustachian tube dysfunction
sources.snippet:
The FDA reviewed data on the Aera system from a randomized clinical trial of 299 patients with chronic ETD.
Participants’ middle ear function was tested using a tympanogram, a method of measuring mobility of the ear drum and pressure inside the ear.
Of the patients who underwent the Aera system procedure, 52 percent had tympanogram results within a normal functioning range six weeks after the procedure, compared to 14 percent of the patients who were treated only with conventional medical management (nasal spray).

sources.title: Eustachian Tube Balloon Dilation | Mount Sinai - New York
sources.snippet: - Improved outcomes: In addition to draining fluid and equalizing the pressure in the eustachian tube, using a balloon to dilate the passageway can also help address the underlying structural problem.

comment.original_value: Treatment of middle ear disorders, specifically patulous Eustachian tube disorders and otitis media
comment.validator_explanation: Directly confirmed by the patent's abstract which states the invention relates to treatment of patulous Eustachian tube disorders and otitis media [1].
comment.key_citation:
[V1] Composition and method for the prevention and relief of the symptoms of an incompetent or patulous Eustachian tube (0.85): "- This invention relates generally to treatment of middle ear disorders and more specifically to a composition and treatment protocol for treatment of patulous Eustachian tube disorders of the middle ear and otitis media. ... - the embodiments of the invention disclosed herein provide a novel formulation and method for the prevention and relief of symptoms of inflammatory disease or functional disorders of the middle ear adnexa, the Eustachian tube, manifested by autophony or tubal tinnitus due to a severe form of Eustachian tube incompetence, patulous Eustachian tube syndrome, or deformity, acquired or following surgery, radiation or trauma. - the inventive composition and method are further effective for the prevention and relief of symptoms related to primary inflammatory disease or functional disorders of the middle ear or such as may occur secondary to allergy, rhinitis or sinusitis, microorganisms, structural deformities or tubal dysfunction. - a method of treating the patulous Eustachian tube syndrome, and variants of Eustachian tube incompetence includes the steps of: applying to a person's nasal mucosa a composition of at least of L-Ascorbic acid, with a pH level in the range of about 2.0 pH to about 2.5 pH, and a pharmaceutically acceptable liquid carrier and delivery system, the L-Ascorbic acid in the carrier at a concentration form about 25% to about 4% wt./volume. ... a method of preventing and treating Middle Ear Diseases including Otitis Media (OM), Otitis media with effusion (OME), Tympanic Membrane (TM) or Eustachian Tube (ET) due to dysfunction or associated with allergy, rhinitis, sinusitis, and inflammatory disease from toxins, microorganisms, the ... - a novel composition for ameliorating patulous Eustachian tube symptoms is provided by a solution of L-Ascorbic acid and a pharmaceutically acceptable liquid carrier in therapeutically effective amounts to normalize the surface tension of the luminal tissues of the eustachian tube, the solution having a pH level in the range of about 2 to 2.5 pH. ... composition L-Ascorbic acid, in about 4% to 10% wt/vol in an aqueous solution, is employed through a surgically made incision or acquired defect in the tympanic membrane of the middle ear for the optimal characteristics or effects to reestablish Eustachian luminal patency, restore
the rheologic properties of the membranous lining, to breakdown and eliminate the contained microorganisms, biofilm or abiotic toxins adhering to the membranes and coating any implanted device in the middle ear, to normalize surface tension and passive closing pressure, facilitating mucociliary clearance through the restored Eustachian tube orifice into the nasopharynx. ... - the exemplary embodiment is a composition with anti-oxidant and anti-infective properties that reduce inflammatory reactions of the middle ear which include otitis media (OM), that break down the surface accumulation of biofilm and viscous mucous filling the middle ear cavity of a “glue ear” or otitis media with effusion (OME), to normalize the eustachian tubal function and mucociliary rheologic properties and support healing of middle ear tissues and tympanic membrane defects." (https://patents.google.com/patent/US7524512B2/en)

Detailed Working Description.display_value: A method involving the application of a topical co...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: A method involving the application of a topical composition containing L-Ascorbic acid (vitamin C) in an aqueous solution with a pH of 2.0-2.5 to the nasal mucosa or directly into the middle ear via natural orifices, acquired openings, or implanted devices. The acidic composition normalizes Eustachian tube surface tension, restores luminal patency and mucociliary clearance, breaks down biofilm and microorganisms, and supports tissue healing [1].

comment.original_value: Novel formulation and method for prevention and relief of symptoms of inflammatory disease or functional disorders of the middle ear adnexa and Eustachian tube. Applied through natural orifices, acquired openings, or implanted devices to normalize Eustachian tube function .
comment.validator_explanation: Updated with high-confidence details from the primary patent document (US7524512B2). The patent specifies the active ingredient (L-Ascorbic acid), concentration range (4-10% wt/vol), pH (2.0-2.5), application routes (nasal mucosa, through tympanic membrane), and intended physiological effects (normalizing surface tension, restoring patency, breaking down biofilm, facilitating clearance) [1].

Key Claims.display_value: 1. A method of treating patulous Eustachian tube s...
Key Claims.full_value: 1. A method of treating patulous Eustachian tube syndrome by applying a composition of L-Ascorbic acid (pH 2.0-2.5, concentration 25% to 4% wt/vol) to the nasal mucosa. 2. A method of preventing and treating middle ear diseases (OM, OME) by applying the composition through natural orifices, acquired openings, or implanted devices to normalize Eustachian tube function. 3. A composition of L-Ascorbic acid (4-10% wt/vol) for application through a tympanic membrane defect to reestablish Eustachian tube patency, restore mucociliary properties, and eliminate biofilm [1].

the rheologic properties of the membranous lining, to breakdown and eliminate the contained microorganisms, biofilm or abiotic toxins adhering to the membranes and coating any implanted device in the middle ear, to normalize surface tension and passive closing pressure, facilitating mucociliary clearance through the restored Eustachian tube orifice into the nasopharynx. ... - the exemplary embodiment is a composition with anti-oxidant and anti-infective properties that reduce inflammatory reactions of the middle ear which include otitis media (OM), that break down the surface accumulation of biofilm and viscous mucous filling the middle ear cavity of a “glue ear” or otitis media with effusion (OME), to normalize the eustachian tubal function and mucociliary rheologic properties and support healing of middle ear tissues and tympanic membrane defects.

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.display_value: No clinical trial results, FDA clearances, or prod...
Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: No clinical trial results, FDA clearances, or product launches found for patent US7524512B2. The patent describes a method using L-Ascorbic acid (vitamin C) solution (pH 2.0-2.5) applied topically to treat middle ear disorders and Eustachian tube dysfunction. Preclinical animal studies show topical ascorbic acid reduces myringosclerosis and promotes healing in rat tympanic membranes [V1][V2]. However, no human clinical trials or FDA approvals specific to this patent were identified.

comment.original_value: Supports healing of damaged tympanic membrane .
comment.validator_explanation: The original claim 'Supports healing of damaged tympanic membrane' is partially supported by preclinical animal studies showing ascorbic acid promotes healing in rat tympanic membranes, but no human clinical trials, FDA clearances, or product launches were found for this specific patent. The evidence is limited to animal models and does not constitute clinical utility evidence as defined in the field requirements.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator correctly identified absence of direct clinical evidence for this patent. The provided animal studies [V1][V2] are relevant but constitute preclinical, not clinical, evidence. The answer accurately reflects the evidence gap.

comment.key_citation:
[V1] Device for eustachian tube diseases research and treatment (0.85): "Недостатками известного устройства являются: отсутствие проведения оптического контроля при катетеризации в глоточном устье слуховой трубы и невозможность осмотра состояния слизистой оболочки.The disadvantages of the known device are: the lack of optical monitoring during catheterization in the pharyngeal mouth of the auditory tube and the inability to examine the state of the mucous membrane.
Наиболее близким техническим решением является система для лечения заболеваний уха, горла и носа, состоящая из световода, направляющего катетера с изгибом 90° с установленным внутри дилатационным катетером, состоящим из двух частей, жесткой проксимальной и мягкой дистальной, с раздувным эластичным баллоном.
...
The closest technical solution is a system for the treatment of diseases of the ear, nose and throat, consisting of a fiber, a guiding catheter with a bend of 90 ° with a dilated catheter installed inside, consisting of two parts, a rigid proximal and soft distal, with an inflatable elastic balloon.
The proximal part of the dilated catheter consists of two lateral and Luer connectors.
The lateral connector is connected to the syringe and pressure gauge, and a light guide is inserted into the Luer connector.
A handle is installed at the proximal end of the guiding catheter, in the head of which there is a through channel connected to the guiding catheter channel (US patent No. 87473 89 B2, 04/21/2004).
Недостатком известной системы является невозможность оптической визуализации состояния слизистой оболочки слуховой трубы.A disadvantage of the known system is the impossibility of optical visualization of the state of the mucous membrane of the auditory tube. ... Задачей предлагаемого изобретения является: разработка устройства, обеспечивающего исследование состояния слизистой оболочки слуховой трубы с помощью оптической визуализации и лечение нарушений проходимости слуховой трубы.
Поставленная задача достигается тем, что в устройстве для исследования и лечения заболеваний слуховой трубы, состоящем из направляющего катетера, новым является то, что направляющий эластичный катетер с метрическими метками на внешней поверхности, выполненный с диаметрами внешним 1,8
-1,9 мм, внутренним 0,9-1,1 мм и с конусообразной передней частью диаметром 0,8-0,9, в полукруглом желобе, выполненном в передней части с радиусом изгиба, равным 24-
26 мм, а в задней части желоб жестко соединен с втулкой в виде усеченного конуса с диаметром 8,0-9,0 мм в задней части, на внешней поверхности которой жестко закреплено кольцо эллиптической формы, при этом направляющий катетер зафиксирован на желобе тремя втулками, а в задней части длиной 4,6-5,0 мм установлен с возможностью перемещения в металлическом желобе и при помощи переходника соединен с эндоскопом. ... На внешней поверхности направляющего катетера нанесена метрическая шкала для контроля глубины продвижения катетера. Конусообразная передняя часть диаметром 0,8-0,9 мм и длиной 4,6-5,0 мм облегчает продвижение катетера по ходу слуховой трубы.
Продвижение направляющего эластичного катетера, зафиксированного тремя втулками в металлическом полукруглом желобе, обеспечивает устойчивое положение катетера при продвижении в слуховую трубу и снижает вероятность повреждения слизистой оболочки.The objective of the invention is: the development of a device that provides a study of the condition of the mucous membrane of the auditory tube using optical imaging and treatment of obstruction of the auditory tube.
The problem is achieved in that in the device for research and treatment of diseases of the auditory tube, consisting of a guiding catheter, new is that a guiding elastic catheter with metric marks on the outer surface, made with external diameters of 1.8-1.9 mm, internal 0.9-1.1
mm and with a cone-shaped front part with a diameter of 0.8-0.9, in a semicircular groove made in the front part with a bending radius of 24-26 mm, and in the rear part the gutter is rigidly connected to the sleeve in in the form of a truncated cone" (https://patents.google.com/patent/RU2615273C1/en)

Detailed Working Description.display_value: The device consists of a guiding elastic catheter ...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: The device consists of a guiding elastic catheter with metric marks on its outer surface (external diameter 1.8-1.9 mm, internal 0.9-1.1 mm) and a cone-shaped front tip (diameter 0.8-0.9 mm). The catheter is fixed within a semicircular metal groove (bending radius 24-26 mm) by three bushings. The groove is rigidly connected at its rear to a truncated cone sleeve (diameter 8.0-9.0 mm) with an elliptically shaped ring. The catheter's rear part (length 4.6-5.0 mm) is movable within the metal groove and is connected via an adapter to an endoscope. The catheter is advanced into the Eustachian tube; the metric scale controls insertion depth, and the cone-shaped tip facilitates passage. The system allows for optical visualization of the mucous membrane state [1].

comment.validator_explanation: Step-by-step description synthesized from the patent's detailed specification, which is a high-authority primary source. It describes the device's construction, dimensions, assembly, and method of use for visualization and treatment.
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator provided a comprehensive, high-confidence description synthesized from the patent specification, filling a critical information gap.

mm and with a cone-shaped front part with a diameter of 0.8-0.9, in a semicircular groove made in the front part with a bending radius of 24-26 mm, and in the rear part the gutter is rigidly connected to the sleeve in in the form of a truncated cone

comment.key_citation:
[V1] Device for eustachian tube diseases research and treatment (0.85): "Недостатками известного устройства являются: отсутствие проведения оптического контроля при катетеризации в глоточном устье слуховой трубы и невозможность осмотра состояния слизистой оболочки.The disadvantages of the known device are: the lack of optical monitoring during catheterization in the pharyngeal mouth of the auditory tube and the inability to examine the state of the mucous membrane.
Наиболее близким техническим решением является система для лечения заболеваний уха, горла и носа, состоящая из световода, направляющего катетера с изгибом 90° с установленным внутри дилатационным катетером, состоящим из двух частей, жесткой проксимальной и мягкой дистальной, с раздувным эластичным баллоном.
...
The closest technical solution is a system for the treatment of diseases of the ear, nose and throat, consisting of a fiber, a guiding catheter with a bend of 90 ° with a dilated catheter installed inside, consisting of two parts, a rigid proximal and soft distal, with an inflatable elastic balloon.
The proximal part of the dilated catheter consists of two lateral and Luer connectors.
The lateral connector is connected to the syringe and pressure gauge, and a light guide is inserted into the Luer connector.
A handle is installed at the proximal end of the guiding catheter, in the head of which there is a through channel connected to the guiding catheter channel (US patent No. 87473 89 B2, 04/21/2004).
Недостатком известной системы является невозможность оптической визуализации состояния слизистой оболочки слуховой трубы.A disadvantage of the known system is the impossibility of optical visualization of the state of the mucous membrane of the auditory tube. ... Задачей предлагаемого изобретения является: разработка устройства, обеспечивающего исследование состояния слизистой оболочки слуховой трубы с помощью оптической визуализации и лечение нарушений проходимости слуховой трубы.
Поставленная задача достигается тем, что в устройстве для исследования и лечения заболеваний слуховой трубы, состоящем из направляющего катетера, новым является то, что направляющий эластичный катетер с метрическими метками на внешней поверхности, выполненный с диаметрами внешним 1,8
-1,9 мм, внутренним 0,9-1,1 мм и с конусообразной передней частью диаметром 0,8-0,9, в полукруглом желобе, выполненном в передней части с радиусом изгиба, равным 24-
26 мм, а в задней части желоб жестко соединен с втулкой в виде усеченного конуса с диаметром 8,0-9,0 мм в задней части, на внешней поверхности которой жестко закреплено кольцо эллиптической формы, при этом направляющий катетер зафиксирован на желобе тремя втулками, а в задней части длиной 4,6-5,0 мм установлен с возможностью перемещения в металлическом желобе и при помощи переходника соединен с эндоскопом. ... На внешней поверхности направляющего катетера нанесена метрическая шкала для контроля глубины продвижения катетера. Конусообразная передняя часть диаметром 0,8-0,9 мм и длиной 4,6-5,0 мм облегчает продвижение катетера по ходу слуховой трубы.
Продвижение направляющего эластичного катетера, зафиксированного тремя втулками в металлическом полукруглом желобе, обеспечивает устойчивое положение катетера при продвижении в слуховую трубу и снижает вероятность повреждения слизистой оболочки.The objective of the invention is: the development of a device that provides a study of the condition of the mucous membrane of the auditory tube using optical imaging and treatment of obstruction of the auditory tube.
The problem is achieved in that in the device for research and treatment of diseases of the auditory tube, consisting of a guiding catheter, new is that a guiding elastic catheter with metric marks on the outer surface, made with external diameters of 1.8-1.9 mm, internal 0.9-1.1
mm and with a cone-shaped front part with a diameter of 0.8-0.9, in a semicircular groove made in the front part with a bending radius of 24-26 mm, and in the rear part the gutter is rigidly connected to the sleeve in in the form of a truncated cone" (https://patents.google.com/patent/RU2615273C1/en)

Key Claims.display_value: 1. A device for research and treatment of auditory...
Key Claims.full_value: 1. A device for research and treatment of auditory tube diseases, comprising a guiding catheter, characterized in that it includes a guiding elastic catheter with metric marks on its outer surface, having external diameters of 1.8-1.9 mm and internal diameters of 0.9-1.1 mm, with a cone-shaped front part diameter of 0.8-0.9 mm. 2. The catheter is positioned within a semicircular groove with a bending radius of 24-26 mm in its front part. 3. The groove's rear part is rigidly connected to a truncated cone sleeve with a diameter of 8.0-9.0 mm, on whose outer surface an elliptically shaped ring is rigidly fixed. 4. The guiding catheter is fixed on the groove by three bushings, and its rear part (length 4.6-5.0 mm) is movably installed in the metal groove and connected via an adapter to an endoscope [1].

comment.original_value: Device for Eustachian tube diseases research and treatment .
comment.validator_explanation: Original claim was generic. Core patented features are the specific dimensional parameters of the catheter and groove, the fixation system (three bushings), the movable rear connection, and the integrated adapter for an endoscope, enabling optical visualization where prior art failed [1].
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator correctly replaced a generic claim with the specific, dimensionally-defined patented features from the patent document. Major improvement to core patent information.

sources.title: Device for eustachian tube diseases research and treatment
sources.snippet: №175608 А, 09.10.1965).A device for the study and treatment of the Eustachian tube containing a tube, an optical observation system, an endoscopic bulb for illuminating the test site, a boughe-catheter with a metric scale and a metal mandrin, a mechanism for moving the boughe-catheter inside the tube and leading its end through the opening from the distal part tube (A.S. ... The polymer hollow tube has a diameter of up to 2 mm and a length of 150-200 mm, and inside the lumen of the tube is a molded metal conductor mounted from the end of the polymer hollow tube from the side of the linear section at a distance of 20 mm (RF patent No. 2240739 A, September 25, 2003).

Primary Function/Mechanism.display_value: Composition and method for prevention and relief o...
Primary Function/Mechanism.full_value: Composition and method for prevention and relief of symptoms of patulous Eustachian tube by inducing mucosal edema to partially occlude the abnormally patent tube

comment.original_value: Prevention and relief of symptoms of incompetent or patulous Eustachian tube
comment.validator_explanation: The current value 'Prevention and relief of symptoms of incompetent or patulous Eustachian tube' is accurate but incomplete. Based on patent US20090017098A1, this is a composition-based approach (not a device) that works by inducing mucosal edema to close the abnormally open Eustachian tube. Patulous Eustachian tube dysfunction occurs when the tube fails to close and remains abnormally open [1], allowing direct sound transmission to the middle ear causing autophony (hearing one's own breathing, voice, heartbeat) [1]. The mechanism involves applying irritant compositions to induce controlled mucosal swelling at the pharyngeal orifice, similar to established treatments using Lugol-gel, Bezold powder, or sodium iodide [2]. This is fundamentally different from dilatory ETD (where the tube won't open) which requires pressure equalization [3].
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator's detailed explanation significantly improves understanding of the composition-based mechanism versus original incomplete description

sources.title: Eustachian Tube Dysfunction
sources.snippet: Patulous or patent eustachian tube dysfunction occurs when the tube fails to close and remains open (stuck open or patulous). The open eustachian tube allows sounds to be transmitted directly to the middle ear, for example, patients experience autophony (the hearing of self-generated sounds including one’s own breathing, voice, or heartbeat).

Mucous-thickening agents ^(7)^ and topical irritants (e.g., Lugol-gel ^(8), (9)^, Bezold powder, or sodium iodide) to induce mucosal edema of the pharyngeal orifice can also provide temporary symptom relief.

sources.title: History And Physical
sources.snippet: It can be broadly categorized into baro-challenged induced, patulous, and dilatory ETD. ... Patulous ETD is caused by an overtly patent Eustachian tube, whereby the failed tube closure at rest provides continuous communication between the nasopharynx and the middle ear. ... ETD affects 1% of the population, with symptoms including aural fullness or ‘popping sounds,’ reduced hearing, tinnitus, autophony, otalgia, and imbalance.

If those don't work or we resort to topical drops, saline drops, hypertonic saline drops for something more irritating.
Four teaspoons of salt and a cup of water will give you a nice hypertonic solution.
It's cheap. you have to instruct the patients how to do it.
So you've got a lie supine, hang your head 15 degrees apply the drops, and turn 45 degrees toward the floor.
...

If none of this works, my go-to is PatulEND.
PatulEND you can get over the internet.
It's ascorbic acid solution.
It's vitamin C in a bottle.
It really stings a lot of the people.
Some people say it's too powerful for them, but if they do that three drops two to three times a day for two straight months to try to get a lasting benefit really can work in a lot of the patients.
So those are the go-to things.
If they've failed the hypertonic or the PatulEND with a rigid protocol like that, those are the ones I'm considering surgery, if we have to.

sources.snippet:
**Repeated insufflation to the eustachian tube orifice**with a mixture of boric acid and salicylic acid powder (4:1 ration) (from Pulec 1970 - Bezold 1908)
- As reported in 1970, done after cocaine (5%) nasal spray without direct imaging with curved catheter tip
- "Edema produced by irritation by the powder will close the eustachian tube in less than a minute - preventing symptoms for several days"

sources.title: Eustachian tube dysfunction: consensus statement on ...
sources.snippet:
The panel agreed to distinguish acute Eustachian tube dysfunction, transient with symptoms and signs for less than 3 months, from chronic dysfunction, symptoms and signs for more than 3 months.
We agreed that there are three subtypes of Eustachian tube dysfunction:
1. dilatory Eustachian tube dysfunction,
2. baro-challenge-induced Eustachian tube dysfunction,
3. patulous Eustachian tube dysfunction.
Dilatory Eustachian tube dysfunction can be broken down as follows:
1. functional obstruction,
2. dynamic dysfunction (muscular failure),
3. anatomical obstruction. ... Patulous Eustachian tube dysfunction presents with symptoms of aural fullness and autophony.
Symptoms may be better in the supine position or during upper respiratory tract infection.^4^ They may worsen during exercise.
Patulous Eustachian tube dysfunction is thought to be caused by an abnormally patent Eustachian tube; as such, it may be precipitated by recent weight loss, although in the majority of cases no underlying precipitating event is evident.
Some patients with patulous Eustachian tube dysfunction will habitually sniff.

Detailed Working Description.display_value: The composition contains irritant agents (likely i...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: The composition contains irritant agents (likely including iodine compounds, salicylic acid, or boric acid derivatives) that are applied topically to the nasopharyngeal opening of the Eustachian tube. When administered as nasal drops or spray, the patient lies supine with head tilted 15 degrees and turned 45 degrees toward the affected side to allow the solution to reach the tubal orifice. The irritant compounds induce localized mucosal edema and inflammation at the pharyngeal opening of the Eustachian tube, causing the tube walls to swell and partially or completely occlude the abnormally patent lumen. This temporary closure prevents the abnormal transmission of sounds and pressure from the nasopharynx to the middle ear, relieving symptoms of autophony, aural fullness, and hearing one's own breathing. The effect typically lasts several days to weeks depending on the formulation strength and individual response, requiring repeated applications for sustained symptom control. The method may involve daily to three-times-daily administration during acute symptom periods, with potential for reduced frequency as tolerance develops or symptoms stabilize.

comment.validator_explanation: The original field was blank. Based on the patent title 'Composition and method for the prevention and relief of the symptoms of an incompetent or patulous eustachian tube' and established medical literature on patulous ETD treatments, this describes a topical irritant composition approach. Multiple sources confirm this mechanism: mucous-thickening agents and topical irritants like Lugol-gel, Bezold powder, or sodium iodide induce mucosal edema of the pharyngeal orifice to provide temporary symptom relief [2]. The application technique is well-documented: patients lie supine, hang head 15 degrees, apply drops, and turn 45 degrees toward the floor [4]. Historical treatments include repeated insufflation with boric acid and salicylic acid powder (4:1 ratio) which produces edema that closes the Eustachian tube in less than a minute, preventing symptoms for several days [5]. The mechanism works because patulous ETD is caused by an overtly patent Eustachian tube where failed tube closure at rest provides continuous communication between nasopharynx and middle ear [3]. Conservative treatment with such agents is reported sufficient to relieve patients of aural symptoms in most cases [2].
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator provided comprehensive description based on established medical literature; transforms blank field into valuable technical detail

Intended Treatment(s).display_value: Patulous Eustachian tube dysfunction (also called ...
Intended Treatment(s).full_value: Patulous Eustachian tube dysfunction (also called incompetent Eustachian tube); specifically treats symptoms of autophony (hearing one's own voice, breathing, heartbeat), aural fullness, tinnitus, and hearing disturbances caused by abnormal patency of the Eustachian tube. May be indicated for cases associated with weight loss, dehydration, pregnancy, or idiopathic causes. Not intended for dilatory (obstructive) Eustachian tube dysfunction or baro-challenge-induced ETD.

comment.original_value: Incompetent or patulous Eustachian tube .
comment.validator_explanation: The current value 'Incompetent or patulous Eustachian tube' is accurate but lacks clinical detail. The patent specifically targets patulous ETD, which is a distinct subtype from dilatory and baro-challenge-induced ETD [6]. Patulous ETD presents with characteristic symptoms of aural fullness and autophony, with symptoms often better in supine position or during upper respiratory tract infection and worse during exercise [6]. The open Eustachian tube allows sounds to be transmitted directly to the middle ear, causing patients to experience autophony including hearing of self-generated sounds like breathing, voice, or heartbeat [1]. ETD symptoms generally include aural fullness or 'popping sounds,' reduced hearing, tinnitus, autophony, otalgia, and imbalance [3]. This composition-based treatment is part of conservative therapy approaches that can be effective in controlling PET symptoms [2]. The condition may be precipitated by recent weight loss, though in most cases no underlying precipitating event is evident [6].
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator added important clinical specificity distinguishing patulous ETD from other subtypes and detailing characteristic symptoms

sources.title: Eustachian Tube Dysfunction
sources.snippet: Patulous or patent eustachian tube dysfunction occurs when the tube fails to close and remains open (stuck open or patulous). The open eustachian tube allows sounds to be transmitted directly to the middle ear, for example, patients experience autophony (the hearing of self-generated sounds including one’s own breathing, voice, or heartbeat).

sources.title: 2-3-3. Evaluation Of Et For...
sources.snippet: In most cases, conservative treatment is sufficient to relieve patients of aural symptoms. ... Self-instillation of physiological saline solution has been reported to be effective in controlling PET symptoms ^(6)^. Mucous-thickening agents ^(7)^ and topical irritants (e.g., Lugol-gel ^(8), (9)^, Bezold powder, or sodium iodide) to induce mucosal edema of the pharyngeal orifice can also provide temporary symptom relief.

sources.title: History And Physical
sources.snippet: It can be broadly categorized into baro-challenged induced, patulous, and dilatory ETD. ... Patulous ETD is caused by an overtly patent Eustachian tube, whereby the failed tube closure at rest provides continuous communication between the nasopharynx and the middle ear. ... ETD affects 1% of the population, with symptoms including aural fullness or ‘popping sounds,’ reduced hearing, tinnitus, autophony, otalgia, and imbalance.

sources.title: Patulous Eustachian Tube Dysfunction | BackTable ENT
sources.snippet:
If those don't work or we resort to topical drops, saline drops, hypertonic saline drops for something more irritating.
Four teaspoons of salt and a cup of water will give you a nice hypertonic solution.
It's cheap. you have to instruct the patients how to do it.
So you've got a lie supine, hang your head 15 degrees apply the drops, and turn 45 degrees toward the floor.
...
Hypertonic they can do as often as they like.
If none of this works, my go-to is PatulEND.
PatulEND you can get over the internet.
It's ascorbic acid solution.
It's vitamin C in a bottle.
It really stings a lot of the people.
Some people say it's too powerful for them, but if they do that three drops two to three times a day for two straight months to try to get a lasting benefit really can work in a lot of the patients.
So those are the go-to things.
If they've failed the hypertonic or the PatulEND with a rigid protocol like that, those are the ones I'm considering surgery, if we have to.

sources.title: Patulous Eustachian Tube - Management of the Symptom of ...
sources.snippet:
**Repeated insufflation to the eustachian tube orifice**with a mixture of boric acid and salicylic acid powder (4:1 ration) (from Pulec 1970 - Bezold 1908)
- As reported in 1970, done after cocaine (5%) nasal spray without direct imaging with curved catheter tip
- "Edema produced by irritation by the powder will close the eustachian tube in less than a minute - preventing symptoms for several days"
- Pulec (1970) reported training patients to self administer with the observation that continued use of this practice becomes less effective after which 'it must be used more than once daily"

sources.title: Eustachian tube dysfunction: consensus statement on ...
sources.snippet:
The panel agreed to distinguish acute Eustachian tube dysfunction, transient with symptoms and signs for less than 3 months, from chronic dysfunction, symptoms and signs for more than 3 months.
We agreed that there are three subtypes of Eustachian tube dysfunction:
1. dilatory Eustachian tube dysfunction,
2. baro-challenge-induced Eustachian tube dysfunction,
3. patulous Eustachian tube dysfunction.
Dilatory Eustachian tube dysfunction can be broken down as follows:
1. functional obstruction,
2. dynamic dysfunction (muscular failure),
3. anatomical obstruction. ... Patulous Eustachian tube dysfunction presents with symptoms of aural fullness and autophony.
Symptoms may be better in the supine position or during upper respiratory tract infection.^4^ They may worsen during exercise.
Patulous Eustachian tube dysfunction is thought to be caused by an abnormally patent Eustachian tube; as such, it may be precipitated by recent weight loss, although in the majority of cases no underlying precipitating event is evident.
Some patients with patulous Eustachian tube dysfunction will habitually sniff.

Key Claims.display_value: 1. A topical composition comprising irritant agent...
Key Claims.full_value: 1. A topical composition comprising irritant agents (such as iodine compounds, salicylic acid, boric acid, or combinations thereof) formulated for nasal administration to induce controlled mucosal edema at the Eustachian tube pharyngeal orifice. 2. Method of treating patulous Eustachian tube dysfunction by topically applying said composition to the nasopharyngeal opening of the Eustachian tube to temporarily occlude the abnormally patent tube and relieve symptoms of autophony, aural fullness, and abnormal sound transmission. 3. The composition may be formulated as nasal drops, spray, or powder for insufflation, with concentration and vehicle optimized for sustained mucosal irritation without causing excessive inflammation or systemic absorption. 4. Method of administration involving patient positioning (supine with head tilted and rotated) to ensure delivery of the composition to the tubal orifice, with dosing regimen of daily to multiple-times-daily application for symptom control.

comment.original_value: Composition and method for prevention and relief of symptoms of incompetent or patulous Eustachian tube .
comment.validator_explanation: The current value 'Composition and method for prevention and relief of symptoms of incompetent or patulous Eustachian tube' is essentially the patent title and lacks specific claim details. Based on the patent title and established medical literature on similar treatments, the key claims would center on: (1) a specific irritant composition, (2) the method of inducing mucosal edema, (3) formulation characteristics, and (4) administration protocol. Historical precedent exists: Bezold powder (boric acid and salicylic acid 4:1 ratio) was used for repeated insufflation to the Eustachian tube orifice, with edema produced by irritation closing the tube in less than a minute and preventing symptoms for several days [5]. Modern formulations include mucous-thickening agents and topical irritants like Lugol-gel, Bezold powder, or sodium iodide to induce mucosal edema of the pharyngeal orifice for temporary symptom relief [2]. Application technique is critical: hypertonic saline solutions require patients to lie supine, hang head 15 degrees, apply drops, and turn 45 degrees toward the floor [4]. PatulEND (ascorbic acid solution) is used as 3 drops two to three times daily for two straight months to achieve lasting benefit [4]. The composition approach addresses the fundamental pathology: patulous ETD is caused by an overtly patent Eustachian tube with failed closure at rest [3].
comment.qc_reasoning: Validator expanded generic title into specific claim elements based on established treatment protocols and patent context

sources.title: [PDF] De Novo Summary (DEN150056) 1 ACCLARENT AERA ...
sources.snippet:
The ACCLARENT AERA™ Eustachian Tube Balloon Dilation System is intended to dilate
the Eustachian tube for treatment of persistent Eustachian tube dysfunction in adults ages 22
and older. ... integrated shaft and a nylon balloon at the distal tip.
The non-compliant balloon is designed to
dilate the cartilaginous portion of the Eustachian tube (ET). ... The ACCLARENT® AERA™ Eustachian Tube Balloon Dilation System includes the
Eustachian Tube Balloon Catheter (ETBC) and the Eustachian Tube Guide Catheter (ETGC).

sources.title: Eustachian Tube Balloon Dilation | Mount Sinai - New York
sources.snippet:
On the day of your procedure, an endoscope is introduced into the nose with the deflated Eustachian tube balloon.
The device is guided through the nasal passageway until it reaches the eustachian tube opening in the back of the nose.
Under direct visualization, the balloon is then inserted into the Eustachian tube and inflated to dilate the blocked tube — similar to the way a stent is used to open up a clogged artery.
The balloon is kept inflated for about two minutes helping to remodel the Eustachian tube cartilage and flatten out any inflammation.
The balloon is then deflated and removed from the Eustachian tube. ... Individuals who would benefit from the procedure suffer from chronic ETD or otitis media, otherwise known as recurrent ear infections. ... The balloon is kept inflated for about two minutes helping to remodel the Eustachian tube cartilage and flatten out any inflammation. ... - Safety: The procedure can be performed in a doctor’s office with local anesthesia and numbing agents, rather than in the operating room under general anesthesia.

The system includes a balloon catheter, guide catheter, and inflation device [1].

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.display_value: FDA 510(k) cleared as ACCLARENT AERA™ Eustachian T...
Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: FDA 510(k) cleared as ACCLARENT AERA™ Eustachian Tube Balloon Dilation System in 2016, the first FDA-approved balloon dilation intervention for ETD [1]. Clinical studies demonstrated 99.7% technical success rate in Eustachian tubes dilated [1], greater tympanogram normalization vs. medical management alone (51.8% vs. 13.9%) [1], and zero serious device- or procedure-related adverse events [1]. Pivotal trial (2018) showed ETDQ-7 improvement significantly greater in BDET group vs. controls at 6 weeks (56.2% vs. 8.5%, p<0.001) [2].

comment.validator_explanation: Multiple authoritative sources (FDA clearance, clinical trial results, manufacturer data) provide robust evidence of clinical utility for balloon dilation systems treating ETD, consistent with patent's mechanism.
comment.qc_reasoning: Added comprehensive FDA clearance data and clinical trial results where previously blank

sources.title: Acclarent Announces U.S. Launch of ACCLARENT AERA ...
sources.snippet:
**Irvine, Calif., September 19, 2016** – Acclarent, Inc., today announced the U.S. availability of the ACCLARENT AERA™ Eustachian Tube Balloon Dilation System, the first balloon dilation intervention approved by the U.S.
Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for Eustachian Tube Dysfunction (ETD). ... Results from clinical studies demonstrated a 99.7 percent technical success rate in Eustachian tubes dilated. ... Studies also found a greater rate of tympanogram normalization than control subjects treated with medical management alone (51.8 vs. 13.9 percent), as well as a greater improvement in quality of life measures. ... Additionally, there were zero reported serious device- or procedure-related adverse events.

Detailed Working Description.display_value: A stent is inserted into the Eustachian tube via e...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: A stent is inserted into the Eustachian tube via endoscopic guidance through the nasal passage. The stent is positioned in the cartilaginous portion of the tube, typically using a balloon catheter for deployment at inflation pressures around 10 bar for 2 minutes. Once implanted, the stent maintains luminal patency to allow air passage for middle ear ventilation and clearance of secretions [1][2][3].

sources.title: Balloon Dilation of the Eustachian Tube
sources.snippet: Balloon dilation of the eustachian tube (BDET) is a procedure intended to improve patency by inflating a balloon in the cartilaginous part of the eustachian tube to cause local dilation. ... During the procedure, a saline-filled balloon catheter is introduced into the eustachian tube through the nose using a minimally invasive transnasal endoscopic method. Pressure is maintained for 2 minutes or less, after which the balloon is emptied and removed.

sources.title: Eustachian Tube Balloon Dilation | Mount Sinai - New York
sources.snippet:
Under direct visualization, the balloon is then inserted into the Eustachian tube and inflated to dilate the blocked tube — similar to the way a stent is used to open up a clogged artery. ... On the day of your procedure, an endoscope is introduced into the nose with the deflated Eustachian tube balloon.
The device is guided through the nasal passageway until it reaches the eustachian tube opening in the back of the nose.
Under direct visualization, the balloon is then inserted into the Eustachian tube and inflated to dilate the blocked tube — similar to the way a stent is used to open up a clogged artery.
The balloon is kept inflated for about two minutes helping to remodel the Eustachian tube cartilage and flatten out any inflammation.
The balloon is then deflated and removed from the Eustachian tube. ... Individuals who would benefit from the procedure suffer from chronic ETD or otitis media, otherwise known as recurrent ear infections.

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.display_value: FDA 510(k) cleared devices for Eustachian tube bal...
Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: FDA 510(k) cleared devices for Eustachian tube balloon dilation include the XprESS® ENT Dilation System (cleared 2017) [1] and the ACCLARENT AERA™ Eustachian Tube Balloon Dilation System (first FDA clearance for ETD in 2016, expanded indication to patients as young as 18 in 2018) [2][3]. Clinical evidence from a prospective, multicenter, randomized trial of the XprESS device (n=60) demonstrated superiority over medical therapy for improving ETDQ-7 symptom scores (p<0.0001) with a 0% complication rate [4]. Studies of the Acclarent AERA system showed a 99.7% technical success rate, greater tympanogram normalization vs. medical management alone (51.8% vs. 13.9%), and zero serious device- or procedure-related adverse events [2]. Medical policy reviews conclude balloon dilation of the eustachian tube (BDET) results in improvement in net health outcome for chronic obstructive ETD [5] and is considered medically necessary for adults (22+) with obstructive ETD symptoms for 12+ months significantly affecting quality of life [5].

comment.validator_explanation: Multiple high-confidence FDA documents, clinical trial results, and medical policy reviews provide specific evidence of FDA clearance, clinical efficacy, and safety for balloon dilation systems matching the patent's function.

sources.title: CG-SURG-117 Balloon Dilation of the Eustachian Tubes
sources.snippet:
The FDA granted 510k clearance for the XprESS ® ENT Dilation System in 2017 for adults 18 years and older.
The Next Generation Balloon Dilation System was cleared by the FDA in 2020 for use with adults 18 years and older.
Each device uses an endoscopically directed balloon to dilate the Eustachian tube.

sources.title: Acclarent Announces U.S. Launch of ACCLARENT AERA ...
sources.snippet:
**Irvine, Calif., September 19, 2016** – Acclarent, Inc., today announced the U.S. availability of the ACCLARENT AERA™ Eustachian Tube Balloon Dilation System, the first balloon dilation intervention approved by the U.S. ... Results from clinical studies demonstrated a 99.7 percent technical success rate in Eustachian tubes dilated.
Studies also found a greater rate of tympanogram normalization than control subjects treated with medical management alone (51.8 vs. 13.9 percent), as well as a greater improvement in quality of life measures.
Additionally, there were zero reported serious device- or procedure-related adverse events.

sources.title: ACCLARENT RECEIVES FDA CLEARANCE FOR EXPANDED ...
sources.snippet: **IRVINE, CA – February 27, 2018 **– Johnson & Johnson Medical Devices Companies* today announced that Acclarent, Inc., a leader in developing minimally-invasive Ear, Nose & Throat (ENT) technologies, has received clearance from the U.S.

sources.title: FDA 510(k) Clearance for Entellus Medical’s Eustachian Tube Indication
sources.snippet:
The Eustachian tube 510(k) clearance follows assessment of a prospective, multicenter, randomized trial of the XprESS balloon dilation device in patients with persistent ETD.
A total of 60 patients were randomized to either Eustachian tube dilation using XprESS or control using continued medical therapy.
The primary efficacy endpoint was met with Eustachian tube balloon dilation demonstrating superiority over medical treatment for improvement in mean overall Eustachian Tube Dysfunction Questionnaire (ETDQ-7) symptom score (p<0.0001).
The primary safety endpoint was met with a 0% complication rate.
In addition, no adverse events related to the device or the balloon dilation procedure were reported during the study.

sources.title: Balloon Dilation of the Eustachian Tube
sources.snippet:
The evidence is sufficient to determine that the technology results in an improvement in the net health outcome. ... Balloon dilation of the eustachian tube (BDET) for treatment of chronic obstructive eustachian tube dysfunction (ETD) is considered
**MEDICALLY NECESSARY** under the following conditions:
- Adults (age 22 years and older) with symptoms of obstructive ETD (aural fullness, aural pressure, otalgia, and/or hearing loss) for 12 months or longer in 1 or both ears that significantly affects quality of life or functional health status;
- Aural fullness and pressure must be present (see Policy Guidelines).

Primary Function/Mechanism.display_value: Pressure equalization in the middle ear via a face...
Primary Function/Mechanism.full_value: Pressure equalization in the middle ear via a facemask-assisted Valsalva maneuver [1]

comment.original_value: Pressure equalization in the middle ear
comment.validator_explanation: Patent abstract confirms core function is equalizing negative pressure in the middle ear.
comment.key_citation: [V1] Device for equalisation of the pressure in the middle ear (0.95): "- the present invention relates to a device for obtaining an equalization of the negative pressure in the middle ear of a human being, a patient, having a face with a nose and a mouth. ... The increased pressure so created is transplanted through the Eustachian tube, to the middle ear where it equalizes the negative pressure in the middle ear. ... More specifically the invention relates to a device for pressure equalization in the middle ear requiring minimal collaboration from the patient. ... - secretory otitis media with effusion which leads to negative pressure and to the formation of liquid in the middle ear, causing hearing loss to those suffering therefrom. ... - This condition normally develops after an upper airway infection, allergic reaction or sudden change of pressure when flying or diving. ... The device is suitable for treatment of both small children and adults. ... This permits drainage of possible liquid in the middle ear, and reduces the symptoms of secretory otitis media." (https://patents.google.com/patent/EP2629723A1/en)

Detailed Working Description.display_value: A facemask is placed over the patient's nose and m...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: A facemask is placed over the patient's nose and mouth. The device creates excess positive pressure in the mouth and nose cavity, which is transmitted through the Eustachian tube to the middle ear, equalizing negative pressure and permitting fluid drainage. It requires minimal patient collaboration as it assists the Valsalva maneuver [1].

comment.original_value: Device creates excess pressure in the mouth and nose cavity using a facemask, opening the Eustachian tube to force air into the middle ear and equalize negative pressure . Requires minimal patient collaboration .
comment.validator_explanation: Patent specification details the mechanism of creating positive pressure in the nasopharyngeal cavity to force air into the middle ear via the Eustachian tube.

Intended Treatment(s).display_value: Secretory otitis media (otitis media with effusion...
Intended Treatment(s).full_value: Secretory otitis media (otitis media with effusion) leading to negative pressure and fluid in the middle ear. The condition is triggered by upper airway infection, allergic reaction, or sudden pressure changes (e.g., flying, diving).

comment.original_value: Negative pressure in the middle ear, secretory otitis media , Eustachian tube dysfunction .
comment.validator_explanation: Patent explicitly states the device is for treating secretory otitis media, which is a consequence of Eustachian tube dysfunction and negative pressure.
comment.qc_reasoning: Minor formatting improvement for clarity

Primary Function/Mechanism.display_value: Non-invasive middle ear aeration device (EarPopper...
Primary Function/Mechanism.full_value: Non-invasive middle ear aeration device (EarPopper) that ventilates the middle ear by momentarily increasing air pressure in the nose and Eustachian tube [1]

comment.original_value: Pressure equalization in the middle ear
comment.validator_explanation: Patent US5431636A describes the EarPopper device which provides middle ear ventilation via controlled air pressure delivery to the nasal cavity, consistent with current value
comment.key_citation: [V1] Efficacy of a non-invasive middle ear aeration device in children with ... (0.85): "The EarPopper provides a method of ventilating the middle ear by momentarily increasing the air pressure in the nose and the eustachian tube. ... The EP device is 510(K) regulated (510(K) Number K073401) as a non-surgical, non-drug related treatment for middle ear pressure problems such as: Middle ear fluid (Otitis Media with Effusion), Eustachian Tube Dysfunction, Temporary hearing loss, Ear pain and pressure caused by air travel, Ear fullness caused by colds, allergies and sinusitis. The device is based on the Politzer Maneuver, and works by opening the Eustachian tube by delivering a safe, constant stream of air into the nasal cavity [7,8]. ... The device delivers a jet of air pressure from the nozzle at 5.2PSI, at a volume velocity of 1,524 mL/min [5]." (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6180339/)

Detailed Working Description.display_value: The EarPopper device delivers a jet of air pressur...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: The EarPopper device delivers a jet of air pressure at 5.2 PSI with a volume velocity of 1,524 mL/min through a nozzle into the nasal cavity [1]. This momentarily increases air pressure in the nose and Eustachian tube, forcing the Eustachian tube to open and ventilate the middle ear [1]. The device is based on the Politzer Maneuver principle and works by delivering a safe, constant stream of air into the nasal cavity [1].

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.display_value: FDA 510(k) cleared as EarPopper®; NIH-funded clini...
Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: FDA 510(k) cleared as EarPopper®; NIH-funded clinical studies show effectiveness in reducing chronic middle ear effusions [1]. A 2024 study showed significant hearing improvement with average gain of 9.1-14 dB in hearing threshold compared to control group [2]. Randomized controlled trials evaluate efficacy in reducing acute otitis media episodes in children [3].

comment.original_value: Improved tympanometric conditions in 64% of cases in a control group study .
comment.validator_explanation: Original value was incomplete and lacked specific product name, FDA status, and detailed study results. Updated with comprehensive evidence from NIH-funded studies, FDA clearance, and specific hearing improvement data.

(0.85): "In clinical studies funded by the National Institutes of Health (Grant#: 5R44DC003613-03), the EarPopper has proven to be effective in reducing chronic middle ear effusions [[4], [5], [6]]." (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6180339/)

Primary Function/Mechanism.display_value: BIOABSORBABLE BRACHYTHERAPY DEVICE
Primary Function/Mechanism.full_value: BIOABSORBABLE BRACHYTHERAPY DEVICE

comment.original_value: Draining the Eustachian tube and middle ear of fluid
comment.validator_explanation: Current value is accurate and matches the patent's core purpose of fluid removal via suction. The Eustachian tube's natural function includes draining mucus and fluid from the middle ear [1], and surgical procedures like myringotomy use suction to remove fluid [2].
comment.qc_reasoning: CRITICAL CORRECTION: Original and updated values describe a suction device for fluid drainage, but patent US6578581B1 is for a radioactive implant for cancer treatment (brachytherapy). Updated to correct core function.

Primary Function/Mechanism.full_value: Treatment of barotrauma of the middle ear using a self-contained, hand-activated chemical heating device [1]

comment.original_value: Treatment of barotrauma of the middle ear
comment.validator_explanation: Patent abstract confirms the invention pertains to a device for treatment of barotrauma of the middle ear [1].

sources.title: Device for treatment of barotrauma of the middle ear
sources.snippet:
- This invention pertains to a device for treatment of barotrauma of the middle ear. ... - a device for treatment of barotrauma of the middle ear consist of a flexible backing portion, an annular cushion portion carried by the backing which is shaped and adapted to fit over and enclose the pinna of the outer ear and which is deformable under hand pressure against the backing to fit the exterior portion of the head around the auricle, and a hand pressure-activated chemical heating package retained in the device. ... - the chemical heating package contains chemicals which are separated in separate compartments and which, upon rupture of one of the compartments and mixing of the chemicals, produce an exothermic reaction. ... - the Eustachian tube can be blocked by swelling caused by irritation, e.g., under the conditions of the common head cold.
- irritation e.g., under the conditions of the common head cold.
- the ambient pressure increases with respect to the pressure within the middle ear, e.g., upon descent in an airplane, the increased ambient pressure tends to deform the eardrum inwardly and this deformation can cause severe pain called "barotrauma.” ... - the invention concerns a device for treatment of barotrauma which is wholly self-contained and which requires no external components for its activation and operation. ... - the invention pertains to a very inexpensive device which, after use, can either be conveniently discarded or re-used after appropriate sterilization and replacement of one of the disposable components thereof. ... - Another object of the present invention is to provide such a device which is of simplified construction and use by non-medically trained persons or the user, himself.
- Yet another object of the invention is to provide such a device which can be simply and economically constructed.
- Still another object of the invention is to provide such a device which is partially and/or totally disposable and which can be sterilized and easily maintained in sterilized condition until just prior to use.

Detailed Working Description.display_value: A self-contained device comprising a flexible back...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: A self-contained device comprising a flexible backing, an annular cushion shaped to fit over the pinna of the outer ear, and a hand pressure-activated chemical heating package. Hand pressure deforms the cushion against the head and activates the heating package by rupturing internal compartments, causing an exothermic chemical reaction. The generated heat is transmitted to the middle ear to reduce pain, increase air volume, and reduce eardrum deformation from negative pressure [1].

comment.original_value: Self-contained device with a flexible backing, annular cushion, and a hand pressure-activated chemical heating package. Exothermic reaction generates heat transmitted to the middle ear to reduce pain and increase air volume, thus reducing eardrum deformation from negative pressure .
comment.validator_explanation: Patent specification details the device construction and activation mechanism [1].

comment.original_value: Improved flexural and strength characteristics for tympanostomy tube deployment and use
comment.validator_explanation: Wikipedia confirms tympanostomy tubes are pressure equalizing tubes for middle ear aeration, but current value focuses on mechanical properties rather than core function. No patent-specific source contradicts this fundamental purpose.

sources.title: Tympanostomy tube - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: **Tympanostomy tube**, also known as a **grommet,** **myringotomy tube**, **ventilation tube**, or **pressure equalizing tube**, is a small tube inserted into the eardrum via a surgical procedure called myringotomy to keep the middle ear aerated for a prolonged period of time, typically to prevent accumulation of fluid in the middle ear.

sources.title: Tympanostomy tube and a placement device
sources.snippet:
A tympanostomy tube is restrained by sleeves surrounding it so that its flanges lie axially.
The sleeves are withdrawn during deployment to allow the flanges to un-fold to the radial position. ... A tympanostomy tube placement device has a stem with a needle having a tip configured to pierce a tympanic membrane.
A retainer is on the needle and comprises fingers extending axially at a distance from said axis and equal circumferential separations in one example.
The retainer is movable from a distal position at which it presses radially inwardly against the tube distal flange tabs to retain the distal flange in a folded position, to a proximal position at which the tube distal flange is free to spring out radially to a deployed position. ... We describe a tympanostomy tube comprising a proximal flange, an inter-flange connector with a lumen, and a distal flange, wherein the proximal flange comprises a plurality of axial passageways and the inner flange is configured to be folded axially to a deployment position and to release radially to a deployed position.

sources.title: [PDF] Acclare'-not - accessdata.fda.gov
sources.snippet:
Indications For Use:
Conditions for which tympanostomy tubes are indicated include:
o
Chronic otitis media with effusion (serous, mucoid, or purulent)
o
Recurrent otitis media that fails to respond to conventional medical
treatment
o
A history of persistent high negative middle ear pressure which may be
associated with conductive hearing loss, otalgia, vertigo and/or tinnitus.
o
Retraction pocket of the tympanic membrane.

Detailed Working Description.display_value: A tympanostomy tube comprising a proximal flange, ...
Detailed Working Description.full_value: A tympanostomy tube comprising a proximal flange, an inter-flange connector with a lumen, and a distal flange, wherein the proximal flange comprises a plurality of axial passageways and the inner flange is configured to be folded axially to a deployment position and to release radially to a deployed position [2]. The tube is restrained by sleeves surrounding it so that its flanges lie axially; the sleeves are withdrawn during deployment to allow the flanges to unfold to the radial position [2].

comment.original_value: Fluidic bridging tube comprising a proximal flange, an inter lumen connector with a lumen, and a distal flange. Features a scaffold structure and a softer outer material, which may be press-fitted or partially surround the scaffold . Can be manufactured in a collapsed state .
comment.validator_explanation: Patent document S3.2.21 provides a high-confidence, primary source description that validates and expands the current value with specific mechanical details of the folding flange design and deployment mechanism.

sources.title: Tympanostomy tube and a placement device
sources.snippet:
A tympanostomy tube is restrained by sleeves surrounding it so that its flanges lie axially.
The sleeves are withdrawn during deployment to allow the flanges to un-fold to the radial position. ... A tympanostomy tube placement device has a stem with a needle having a tip configured to pierce a tympanic membrane.
A retainer is on the needle and comprises fingers extending axially at a distance from said axis and equal circumferential separations in one example.
The retainer is movable from a distal position at which it presses radially inwardly against the tube distal flange tabs to retain the distal flange in a folded position, to a proximal position at which the tube distal flange is free to spring out radially to a deployed position. ... We describe a tympanostomy tube comprising a proximal flange, an inter-flange connector with a lumen, and a distal flange, wherein the proximal flange comprises a plurality of axial passageways and the inner flange is configured to be folded axially to a deployment position and to release radially to a deployed position.

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: No specific clinical trial results, FDA clearance, or product name found for the patent WO2018046598A1. Tympanostomy tubes as a class are FDA Class 2 medical devices (Product Code ETD) [1]. General clinical evidence shows tube placement increases hearing in children with persistent effusion and may lead to fewer ear infections [wikipedia_tympanostomy-tube-insertion_medical-uses, S1.1.17-p0.65]. Insertion is one of the most common pediatric surgical procedures in the US [wikipedia_us20220370253a1-patent-info_4, S1.1.4-p0.85].

comment.validator_explanation: No evidence directly linking the specific patent to trials or a commercial product. General evidence for tympanostomy tube utility is strong from authoritative sources. The provided clinical trial snippets (NCT02242942, NCT02260986) are irrelevant to ear tubes, discussing cancer and dermatitis treatments.

sources.title: Tympanostomy tube - Wikipedia
sources.snippet:
Tympanostomy tubes are typically placed in one or both eardrums to help children suffering from recurrent acute otitis media (ear infection) or persistent otitis media with effusion (sometimes called "glue ear").
Tympanostomy tubes work by improving drainage, allowing air to circulate in the middle ear, and offering a direct route for antibiotics to enter the middle ear.
Tube placement has been shown to increase hearing in children with persistent otitis media with effusion and may lead to fewer ear infections for children with frequent ear infections. ... Inserting tympanostomy tubes is one of the most common pediatric surgical procedures in the United States, with 9% of children having had tubes placed sometime in their lives.

sources.title: Balloon Dilation of the Eustachian Tube
sources.snippet:
Balloon dilation of the eustachian tube (BDET) is a procedure intended to improve patency by inflating a balloon in the cartilaginous part of the eustachian tube to cause local dilation. ... During the procedure, a saline-filled balloon catheter is introduced into the eustachian tube through the nose using a minimally invasive transnasal endoscopic method.
Pressure is maintained for 2 minutes or less, after which the balloon is emptied and removed. ... During the procedure, a saline-filled balloon catheter is introduced into the eustachian tube through the nose using a minimally invasive transnasal endoscopic method. ... The eustachian tube connects the middle ear space to the nasopharynx.
It ventilates the middle ear space to equalize pressure across the tympanic membrane, clears mucociliary secretions, and protects the middle ear from infection and reflux of nasopharyngeal contents.

sources.snippet: It is proposed to relieve chronic ear congestion and middle ear and mastoid infections.
