## OTO-201 for the Treatment of Middle Ear Effusion in Pediatric Subjects Requiring Tympanostomy Tube PlacementClinicalTrials.gov ID
## Study Overview
This is a prospective, randomized, double-blind, sham-controlled, multicenter, Phase 3 study of OTO-201 administered intraoperatively in pediatric subjects with bilateral middle ear effusion requiring TT (tympanostomy tube) placement. ... This is a prospective, randomized, double-blind, sham-controlled, multicenter, Phase 3 study of OTO-201 administered intraoperatively in pediatric subjects with bilateral middle ear effusion requiring TT (tympanostomy tube) placement.
One dose level (6 mg) of OTO-201 was evaluated relative to sham (empty syringe).
Study subjects are required to have a clinical diagnosis of bilateral middle ear effusion requiring TT placement. One dose level (6 mg) of OTO-201 was evaluated relative to sham (empty syringe). ... - Subject is a male or female aged 6 months to 17 years, inclusive
- Subject has a clinical diagnosis of bilateral middle ear effusion requiring tympanostomy tube placement
- Subject's caregiver is willing to comply with the protocol and attend all study visits ... |Participant Group/Arm|Intervention/Treatment|
|Participant Group/Arm|OTO-201: Single, intratympanic injection: One injection of 6 mg OTO-201 (ciprofloxacin in poloxamer 407) into each ear during surgery.|Intervention/Treatment||
|Participant Group/Arm|Sham: Simulated single, intratympanic injection: One sham injection into each ear during surgery.|Intervention/Treatment|| ... |Participant Group/Arm|Intervention/Treatment|
|Percentage of Participants Who Were Treatment Failures.|Cumulative proportion of treatment failures: The efficacy endpoint for both trials was the cumulative proportion of study treatment failures through Day 15, defined as the occurrence of any of the following events: otorrhea as determined by a blinded assessor on or after 3 days post-surgery, otic or systemic antibacterial drug use for any reason any time post-surgery, as well as patients who missed visits or were lost-to-follow-up.|Day 15 - 2 weeks after dosing| ... |Participant Group/Arm|Intervention/Treatment|
|Evaluation of Adverse Events, Otoscopic Exams, Audiometry and Tympanometry|Safety variables included the frequency of adverse events (AEs) and results from otoscopic examinations, tympanometry, audiometry measurements.|Up to one month| |Participant Group/Arm|Intervention/Treatment|
|Microbiological Response|Subjects whose samples tested positive for bacteria in either or both ears at baseline with documented eradication or presumed eradication post-baseline.|Day 15 - 2 weeks after dosing|" (https://clinicaltrials.gov/search?cond=Otology&intr=Device)

Key Claims.display_value: 1. Method of treating middle ear effusion via sing...
Key Claims.full_value: 1. Method of treating middle ear effusion via single intratympanic injection of sustained-release antibiotic formulation during tympanostomy tube surgery. 2. Reduction in treatment failures (otorrhea, antibiotic use) through Day 15 post-surgery. 3. Microbiological eradication of baseline bacterial infections. 4. Safety profile assessment through otoscopic exams, audiometry, and tympanometry. [1]

Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.display_value: Phase 3 clinical trial (NCT01949155) results: Cumu...
Scientific/Clinical Utility Evidence.full_value: Phase 3 clinical trial (NCT01949155) results: Cumulative proportion of treatment failures through Day 15 (otorrhea, antibiotic use, missed visits) was primary efficacy endpoint [clinicaltrials_ciprofloxacin-poloxamer-407_treatment-failures, S1.1.35-p0.95]. Safety assessments included adverse events, otoscopic exams, audiometry, and tympanometry [clinicaltrials_ciprofloxacin-poloxamer-407_safety-variables, S1.1.36-p0.95]. Microbiological response measured eradication of baseline bacterial infections [clinicaltrials_ciprofloxacin-poloxamer-407_microbiological-response, S1.1.37-p0.95].

general_notes: This table catalogs patents for devices and methods controlling ear canal air pressure, used for conditions like otitis media, Eustachian tube dysfunction, and barotitis media. Research purpose: understand device function, success, and scientific utility. Include international patents. Key context from research: Search patent databases (USPTO, EPO Espacenet, WIPO PATENTSCOPE) using keywords 'ear pressure equalization,' 'Eustachian tube dysfunction,' and IPC/CPC classification A61F11/00. Pressure equalization tubes (tympanostomy) are standard; auto-inflation devices like Otovent provide non-surgical relief. Clinical studies show device interventions dominate otology trials (43.6% of 915 trials 2019-2025). Commercial otorhinolaryngology market projected to grow from $27.3B (2024) to $37.72B (2030). Validate all columns thoroughly; prioritize recent patent status and clinical evidence where available.
